298: div.relative:has(img[data-nimg="fill"]) {
299:   min-height: 300px !important;
300:   position: relative !important;
301:   width: 100%;
302: }

306: }

================
File: src/types/supabase.ts
================
 1: export type Json =
 2:   | string
 3:   | number
 4:   | boolean
 5:   | null
 6:   | { [key: string]: Json | undefined }
 7:   | Json[]
 8: export interface Database {
 9:   public: {
10:     Tables: {
11:       pledges: {
12:         Row: {
13:           id: string
14:           name: string
15:           email: string
16:           comment: string | null
17:           verified: boolean
18:           verification_token: string
19:           created_at: string
20:           verified_at: string | null
21:           ip_address: string | null
22:           user_agent: string | null
23:           locale: string | null
24:         }
25:         Insert: {
26:           id?: string
27:           name: string
28:           email: string
29:           comment?: string | null
30:           verified?: boolean
31:           verification_token?: string
32:           created_at?: string
33:           verified_at?: string | null
34:           ip_address?: string | null
35:           user_agent?: string | null
36:           locale?: string | null
37:         }
38:         Update: {
39:           id?: string
40:           name?: string
41:           email?: string
42:           comment?: string | null
43:           verified?: boolean
44:           verification_token?: string
45:           created_at?: string
46:           verified_at?: string | null
47:           ip_address?: string | null
48:           user_agent?: string | null
49:           locale?: string | null
50:         }
51:         Relationships: []
52:       }
53:     }
54:     Views: {
55:       [_ in never]: never
56:     }
57:     Functions: {
58:       get_verified_pledge_count: {
59:         Args: Record<PropertyKey, never>
60:         Returns: number
61:       }
62:       verify_pledge: {
63:         Args: {
64:           token: string
65:         }
66:         Returns: boolean
67:       }
68:     }
69:     Enums: {
70:       [_ in never]: never
71:     }
72:     CompositeTypes: {
73:       [_ in never]: never
74:     }
75:   }
76: }

================
File: supabase/pledges_schema.sql
================
 1: -- Schema for the pledges table in Supabase
 2: -- Run this SQL in your Supabase SQL editor
 3: -- Create the pledges table
 4: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.pledges (
 5:   id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
 6:   name TEXT NOT NULL,
 7:   email TEXT NOT NULL,
 8:   comment TEXT,
 9:   verified BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
10:   verification_token UUID NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
11:   created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
12:   verified_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
13:   ip_address TEXT,
14:   user_agent TEXT,
15:   locale TEXT
16: );
17: -- Create an index on email for faster lookups
18: CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS pledges_email_idx ON public.pledges (email);
19: -- Create an index on verification token for faster lookups
20: CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS pledges_verification_token_idx ON public.pledges (verification_token);
21: -- Add RLS (Row Level Security) policies
22: ALTER TABLE public.pledges ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
23: -- Create policy for anonymous users to insert but not read all records
24: CREATE POLICY "Allow inserts for anonymous users" 
25:   ON public.pledges FOR INSERT 
26:   TO anon
27:   WITH CHECK (true);
28: -- Create policy for allowing read access only to verified pledges for display purposes
29: CREATE POLICY "Allow reading verified pledges" 
30:   ON public.pledges FOR SELECT 
31:   TO anon
32:   USING (verified = true);
33: -- Create policy for service role to read/update all records (for admin)
34: CREATE POLICY "Service role can do everything" 
35:   ON public.pledges
36:   TO service_role
37:   USING (true)
38:   WITH CHECK (true);
39: -- Create function to get count of verified pledges
40: CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION get_verified_pledge_count()
41: RETURNS INTEGER AS
42: $$
43:   SELECT COUNT(*) FROM public.pledges WHERE verified = true;
44: $$
45: LANGUAGE SQL;
46: -- Create function to verify a pledge by token
47: CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION verify_pledge(token UUID)
48: RETURNS BOOLEAN AS
49: $$
50: DECLARE
51:   updated_rows INTEGER;
52: BEGIN
53:   UPDATE public.pledges
54:   SET verified = true, verified_at = now()
55:   WHERE verification_token = token AND verified = false;
56:   GET DIAGNOSTICS updated_rows = ROW_COUNT;
57:   RETURN updated_rows > 0;
58: END;
59: $$
60: LANGUAGE plpgsql;

================
File: supabase/README.md
================
 1: # Supabase Setup for the Cancel-Wolt Project
 2: 
 3: This directory contains the database schema and setup instructions for the Supabase backend of the Cancel-Wolt project.
 4: 
 5: ## Getting Started
 6: 
 7: 1. Create a Supabase account at https://supabase.com if you don't have one already
 8: 2. Create a new project in Supabase
 9: 3. After the project is created, note your project URL and anon key from the API settings
10: 
11: ## Setting Up the Database
12: 
13: 1. Navigate to the SQL Editor in your Supabase dashboard
14: 2. Create a new query and paste in the contents of `schema.sql` from this directory
15: 3. Execute the query to create the necessary tables and functions
16: 
17: ## Environment Variables
18: 
19: Add the following environment variables to your Vercel project or local `.env.local` file:
20: 
21: ```
22: NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=https://your-project-id.supabase.co
23: NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=your-anon-key
24: ```
25: 
26: Replace `your-project-id` and `your-anon-key` with your actual Supabase project details.
27: 
28: ## Email Service Setup
29: 
30: For email verification to work properly, you will need to set up an email service. The project is currently configured to use a simple console log for development, but for production, you should configure an email provider.
31: 
32: ### Option 1: SMTP Configuration
33: 
34: Set up the following environment variables:
35: 
36: ```
37: EMAIL_SERVER_HOST=smtp.example.com
38: EMAIL_SERVER_PORT=587
39: EMAIL_SERVER_USER=your-username
40: EMAIL_SERVER_PASSWORD=your-password
41: EMAIL_FROM=noreply@example.com
42: ```
43: 
44: ### Option 2: Email Service Integration
45: 
46: Alternatively, you can use a transactional email service like SendGrid, Mailgun, or AWS SES.
47: 
48: ## Security Considerations
49: 
50: - For production, make sure to implement proper email verification
51: - Consider implementing rate limiting for pledge submissions
52: - Regularly backup your database
53: - Use environment variables for sensitive information and do not commit them to version control
54: 
55: ## Tables & Structure
56: 
57: ### Pledges Table
58: 
59: - `id`: UUID, primary key
60: - `created_at`: Timestamp of creation
61: - `name`: User's name
62: - `email`: User's email (unique)
63: - `comment`: Optional comment from the user
64: - `verified`: Boolean to track email verification status
65: - `verification_token`: UUID used for email verification
66: 
67: ## Row Level Security (RLS)
68: 
69: The database uses RLS policies to ensure:
70: 
71: 1. Anyone can submit a new pledge
72: 2. Only verified pledges are visible publicly
73: 3. Updates to a pledge require the correct verification token
74: 
75: ## Functions
76: 
77: - `get_verified_pledge_count()`: Returns the count of verified pledges

================
File: supabase/schema.sql
================
 1: -- Create pledges table for storing user signatures
 2: CREATE TABLE pledges (
 3:   id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
 4:   created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
 5:   name TEXT NOT NULL,
 6:   email TEXT NOT NULL,
 7:   comment TEXT,
 8:   verified BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
 9:   verification_token UUID NOT NULL,
10:   -- Add constraints
11:   CONSTRAINT email_unique UNIQUE (email)
12: );
13: -- Enable Row Level Security
14: ALTER TABLE pledges ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
15: -- Create policy to allow inserts from anyone (for new pledges)
16: CREATE POLICY "Allow inserts to pledges" 
17:   ON pledges FOR INSERT 
18:   WITH CHECK (true);
19: -- Create policy to allow reads of verified pledges by anyone
20: CREATE POLICY "Allow anyone to read verified pledges" 
21:   ON pledges FOR SELECT 
22:   USING (verified = true);
23: -- Create policy to allow updates only with verification token
24: CREATE POLICY "Allow updates with verification token" 
25:   ON pledges FOR UPDATE 
26:   USING (true)
27:   WITH CHECK (verification_token::text = current_setting('request.jwt.claims', true)::json->>'verification_token');
28: -- Create function to get total verified pledge count
29: CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION get_verified_pledge_count()
30: RETURNS INT 
31: LANGUAGE SQL SECURITY DEFINER
32: AS $$
33:   SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pledges WHERE verified = true;
34: $$;

================
File: .cursor/rules/mcp-rules.mdc
================
  1: ---
  2: description: 
  3: globs: 
  4: alwaysApply: true
  5: ---
  6: You Must use the MCP Think Tank to extend your AI assistant with structured reasoning, persistent memory, task management, and web research capabilities. 
  7: 
  8: ### 1. Thinking Tools
  9: - `think` - Use for complex reasoning, architecture planning, and step-by-step problem-solving
 10:   - **When to use**: For important decisions requiring structured analysis or reflection
 11:   - **Pro tip**: Add `storeInMemory: true` to save important thoughts in the knowledge graph
 12:   - **Example**: `think({structuredReasoning: "Analyzing pros/cons of database options...", storeInMemory: true})`
 13: 
 14: ### 2. Memory & Knowledge Graph
 15: - `create_entities` - Add new concepts to the knowledge graph
 16:   - **When to use**: To document important concepts, systems, or components
 17:   - **Example**: `create_entities([{name: "AuthSystem", entityType: "System", observations: ["Uses JWT"]}])`
 18: 
 19: - `add_observations` - Add facts to existing entities
 20:   - **When to use**: When new information about an existing entity is discovered
 21:   - **Example**: `add_observations([{entityName: "AuthSystem", contents: ["Expires tokens after 24h"]}])`
 22: 
 23: - `create_relations` - Create relationships between entities
 24:   - **When to use**: To connect related concepts in the knowledge graph
 25:   - **Example**: `create_relations([{from: "AuthSystem", to: "Security", relationType: "enhances"}])`
 26: 
 27: - `read_graph` - Get the entire knowledge graph
 28:   - **When to use**: To understand the full project context before making major decisions
 29: 
 30: - `search_nodes` - Find entities matching search criteria
 31:   - **When to use**: To retrieve relevant knowledge before solving a problem
 32:   - **Example**: `search_nodes({query: "authentication"})`
 33: 
 34: - `open_nodes` - Retrieve specific entities by name
 35:   - **When to use**: When you need details about a specific known entity
 36:   - **Example**: `open_nodes({names: ["AuthSystem"]})`
 37: 
 38: - `update_entities` - Modify existing entities
 39:   - **When to use**: When core information about an entity changes
 40:   - **Example**: `update_entities([{name: "AuthSystem", entityType: "Security"}])`
 41: 
 42: - `update_relations` - Modify relationships between entities
 43:   - **When to use**: When the nature of a relationship changes
 44:   - **Example**: `update_relations([{from: "AuthSystem", to: "Security", relationType: "implements"}])`
 45: 
 46: - `delete_entities` - Remove entities from the knowledge graph
 47:   - **When to use**: When concepts become obsolete or are merged
 48:   - **Example**: `delete_entities({entityNames: ["OldSystem"]})`
 49: 
 50: - `delete_observations` - Remove specific facts from entities
 51:   - **When to use**: When information becomes outdated or incorrect
 52:   - **Example**: `delete_observations([{entityName: "AuthSystem", observations: ["Uses MD5"]}])`
 53: 
 54: - `delete_relations` - Remove relationships between entities
 55:   - **When to use**: When connections are no longer valid
 56:   - **Example**: `delete_relations([{from: "AuthSystem", to: "Legacy", relationType: "depends on"}])`
 57: 
 58: ### 3. Task Management
 59: - `plan_tasks` - Create and organize project tasks
 60:   - **When to use**: At project start or when planning new features
 61:   - **Example**: `plan_tasks([{description: "Implement login", priority: "high"}])`
 62: 
 63: - `list_tasks` - Retrieve tasks filtered by status/priority
 64:   - **When to use**: To understand current work status
 65:   - **Example**: `list_tasks({status: "todo"})`
 66: 
 67: - `next_task` - Get and start the highest priority task
 68:   - **When to use**: When ready to work on the next priority
 69:   - **Example**: `next_task({random_string: ""})`
 70: 
 71: - `complete_task` - Mark tasks as done
 72:   - **When to use**: When a task is finished
 73:   - **Example**: `complete_task({id: "task-uuid"})`
 74: 
 75: - `update_tasks` - Modify existing tasks
 76:   - **When to use**: When priorities change or task details need updating
 77:   - **Example**: `update_tasks([{id: "task-uuid", priority: "high"}])`
 78: 
 79: - `show_memory_path` - Get the location of the knowledge graph file
 80:   - **When to use**: For debugging or when examining stored knowledge directly
 81:   - **Example**: `show_memory_path({random_string: ""})`
 82: 
 83: ### 4. When user mentions "Research"
 84: When the user mentions research, use the following four tools. 
 85: - `exa_search` - Search the web for information
 86:   - **When to use**: When current context is insufficient and you need external facts
 87:   - **Example**: `exa_search({query: "latest React state management"})`
 88: 
 89: - `exa_answer` - Get sourced answers to specific questions
 90:   - **When to use**: For factual questions requiring cited sources
 91:   - **Example**: `exa_answer({question: "What is quantum computing?"})`
 92: 
 93: - `resolve-library-id` -  to retrieve a valid Context7-compatible library ID.
 94: Parameters: libraryName: Library name to search for and retrieve a Context-compatible library ID.
 95: 
 96: - `get-library-docs` - Fetches up-to-date documentation for a library. You must call 'resolve-library-id' first to obtain the
 97: exact Context/-compatible library ID required to use this tool.
 98: Parameters:
 99: • context7CompatibleLibraryID: Exact Context7-compatible library ID (e.g., 'mongodb/docs',
100: 'vercel/nextjs') retrieved from 'resolve-library-id'.
101: • topic: Topic to focus documentation on (e.g., 'hooks', 'routing").
102: 
103: ## Best Practices
104: 
105: ### Thinking Process
106: - Use the `think` tool for all complex decisions to ensure thorough analysis
107: - Structure your reasoning with clear problem definition, context, analysis, and conclusion
108: - Set `storeInMemory: true` for important decisions that should be accessible later
109: 
110: ### Knowledge Management
111: - Before creating new entities, search the graph for related concepts
112: - Create a clear entity type hierarchy and consistent naming
113: - Link related entities to build a comprehensive knowledge web
114: - Regularly update entities with new observations as the project evolves
115: 
116: ### Task Workflow
117: 1. Use `plan_tasks` to break down features into clear steps
118: 2. Use `list_tasks` to review current priorities
119: 3. Use `next_task` to identify what to work on next
120: 4. Use `complete_task` when finished with an item
121: 5. Use `update_tasks` to adjust priorities as needs change
122: 
123: ### Research Protocol
124: - Always cite sources when using web research tools
125: - Save important findings to the knowledge graph
126: - Combine research with structured thinking for best results
127: 
128: ## How To Save Important Thoughts
129: When using the `think` tool with important reasoning, set `storeInMemory: true` or simply tell the agent "Please save this reasoning in memory for future reference."

================
File: src/app/about/page.tsx
================
 1: 'use client';
 2: import PageHero from '@/components/PageHero';
 3: import ContentSection from '@/components/ContentSection';
 4: import Citation from '@/components/Citation';
 5: import Link from 'next/link';
 6: export default function AboutPage() {
 7:   return (
 8:     <>
 9:       <PageHero 
10:         title="About the Movement"
11:         subtitle="A grassroots initiative to promote fair food delivery in Denmark"
12:         color="secondary"
13:       />
14:       <main>
15:         <ContentSection 
16:           title="Our Mission"
17:           bgColor="white"
18:         >
19:           <p className="mb-4">
20:             The Cancel Wolt initiative is a grassroots movement created by concerned Danish consumers, restaurant owners, and delivery workers who believe in fair business practices within the food delivery ecosystem.
21:           </p>
22:           <p className="mb-4">
23:             We aim to raise awareness about the hidden costs of convenience and work toward a more equitable food delivery market that respects:
24:           </p>
25:           <ul className="list-disc pl-6 mb-6 space-y-2">
26:             <li>Fair pricing for consumers</li>
27:             <li>Reasonable commissions for restaurants</li>
28:             <li>Proper worker protections for couriers</li>
29:             <li>Transparent business practices</li>
30:           </ul>
31:           <p>
32:             This is not about destroying food delivery services—it's about making them better for everyone involved. We believe that consumers, when informed, will make choices that align with their values.
33:           </p>
34:         </ContentSection>
35:         <ContentSection 
36:           title="Why This Matters"
37:           bgColor="light"
38:         >
39:           <p className="mb-4">
40:             <strong>For Consumers:</strong> You deserve to know the true cost of your food delivery and how your choices impact your community. When you order through Wolt, you're paying significantly more than you would ordering directly from restaurants—not just through delivery fees, but through inflated menu prices needed to cover Wolt's high commissions.
41:           </p>
42:           <p className="mb-4">
43:             <strong>For Restaurants:</strong> Small local establishments are struggling with razor-thin margins that get squeezed even further by massive commissions. In Aalborg, over 30 restaurants boycotted Wolt in 2022<Citation id="1" source="Aalborg Restaurant Boycott Report, 2022" /> because they "couldn't make ends meet" with Wolt's 25-30% cut of every sale.
44:           </p>
45:           <p>
46:             <strong>For Workers:</strong> Delivery couriers deserve basic protections that are standard in Danish employment—sick pay, holiday pay, pension contributions, and insurance. Wolt's "partner" model sidesteps these responsibilities while pushing couriers to deliver faster for competitive pay rates.
47:           </p>
48:         </ContentSection>
49:         <ContentSection 
50:           title="How You Can Help"
51:           bgColor="white"
52:         >
53:           <div className="space-y-6">
54:             <div>
55:               <h3 className="text-xl font-semibold mb-2">1. Sign the Pledge</h3>
56:               <p>
57:                 Commit to avoiding Wolt and choosing fairer alternatives. Each pledge sends a message that Danish consumers care about fair business practices.
58:               </p>
59:             </div>
60:             <div>
61:               <h3 className="text-xl font-semibold mb-2">2. Spread the Word</h3>
62:               <p>
63:                 Share this website with friends, family, and on social media. The more people know about these issues, the more impact we can have.
64:               </p>
65:             </div>
66:             <div>
67:               <h3 className="text-xl font-semibold mb-2">3. Order Directly</h3>
68:               <p>
69:                 When possible, order directly from restaurants through their websites or by phone. This ensures they receive 100% of your payment.
70:               </p>
71:             </div>
72:             <div>
73:               <h3 className="text-xl font-semibold mb-2">4. Support Our Alternatives</h3>
74:               <p>
75:                 Use our alternatives finder to discover delivery options that treat workers and restaurants more fairly.
76:               </p>
77:             </div>
78:           </div>
79:           <div className="mt-8 flex flex-wrap gap-4">
80:             <Link 
81:               href="/pledge"
82:               className="inline-block px-6 py-3 bg-wolt-blue hover:bg-wolt-dark text-white rounded-lg font-medium transition duration-200"
83:             >
84:               Sign the Pledge
85:             </Link>
86:             <Link 
87:               href="/alternatives"
88:               className="inline-block px-6 py-3 bg-gray-200 hover:bg-gray-300 dark:bg-gray-700 dark:hover:bg-gray-600 text-gray-900 dark:text-white rounded-lg font-medium transition duration-200"
89:             >
90:               Find Alternatives
91:             </Link>
92:           </div>
93:         </ContentSection>
94:       </main>
95:     </>
96:   );
97: }

================
File: src/app/alternatives/page.tsx
================
  1: 'use client';
  2: import { useState } from 'react';
  3: import PageHero from '@/components/PageHero';
  4: import ContentSection from '@/components/ContentSection';
  5: import Link from 'next/link';
  6: export default function AlternativesPage() {
  7:   const [city, setCity] = useState('');
  8:   return (
  9:     <>
 10:       <PageHero 
 11:         title="Find Better Alternatives"
 12:         subtitle="Support restaurants and workers by choosing fairer delivery options"
 13:         color="secondary"
 14:       />
 15:       <main>
 16:         <section className="py-12 bg-white dark:bg-gray-900">
 17:           <div className="container mx-auto px-4">
 18:             <div className="max-w-3xl mx-auto text-center mb-12">
 19:               <h2 className="text-3xl font-bold mb-6 text-gray-900 dark:text-white">Restaurant Finder</h2>
 20:               <p className="text-lg mb-8 text-gray-700 dark:text-gray-300">
 21:                 Enter your city to find restaurants that offer direct ordering or use more fair delivery services.
 22:               </p>
 23:               <div className="flex flex-col md:flex-row gap-4 justify-center">
 24:                 <input
 25:                   type="text"
 26:                   value={city}
 27:                   onChange={(e) => setCity(e.target.value)}
 28:                   placeholder="Enter your city..."
 29:                   className="px-4 py-3 rounded-lg border border-gray-300 dark:border-gray-700 bg-white dark:bg-gray-800 text-gray-900 dark:text-white focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-wolt-blue md:w-64"
 30:                 />
 31:                 <button 
 32:                   className="px-6 py-3 bg-wolt-blue hover:bg-wolt-dark text-white rounded-lg font-medium transition duration-200"
 33:                 >
 34:                   Search
 35:                 </button>
 36:               </div>
 37:               <p className="text-sm mt-4 text-gray-500 dark:text-gray-400">
 38:                 Coming soon: Integration with Just Eat API to show actual restaurant options
 39:               </p>
 40:             </div>
 41:           </div>
 42:         </section>
 43:         <ContentSection 
 44:           title="Why Choose Alternatives?"
 45:           bgColor="light"
 46:         >
 47:           <div className="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 gap-8">
 48:             <div>
 49:               <h3 className="text-xl font-semibold mb-4">For Restaurants</h3>
 50:               <ul className="list-disc pl-6 mb-6 space-y-2">
 51:                 <li>Keep more of your money with lower commission rates</li>
 52:                 <li>Direct ordering means 100% of revenue stays with the restaurant</li>
 53:                 <li>More flexible pricing options without price parity clauses</li>
 54:                 <li>Better control over customer relationship and experience</li>
 55:               </ul>
 56:             </div>
 57:             <div>
 58:               <h3 className="text-xl font-semibold mb-4">For Workers</h3>
 59:               <ul className="list-disc pl-6 mb-6 space-y-2">
 60:                 <li>Just Eat has signed a union agreement with 3F</li>
 61:                 <li>Better working conditions and protections</li>
 62:                 <li>Restaurant employees often have more stable employment</li>
 63:                 <li>Support the Danish model of fair labor practices</li>
 64:               </ul>
 65:             </div>
 66:           </div>
 67:         </ContentSection>
 68:         <ContentSection 
 69:           title="Ways to Order Directly"
 70:           bgColor="white"
 71:         >
 72:           <p className="mb-6">
 73:             Here are several ways you can order directly from restaurants and support your local businesses:
 74:           </p>
 75:           <div className="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 gap-6">
 76:             <div className="bg-white dark:bg-gray-800 p-6 rounded-lg shadow-md border border-gray-200 dark:border-gray-700">
 77:               <h3 className="text-xl font-semibold mb-3">Restaurant Websites</h3>
 78:               <p className="text-gray-700 dark:text-gray-300">
 79:                 Many restaurants have their own online ordering systems. Check the restaurant's website first to see if you can order directly.
 80:               </p>
 81:             </div>
 82:             <div className="bg-white dark:bg-gray-800 p-6 rounded-lg shadow-md border border-gray-200 dark:border-gray-700">
 83:               <h3 className="text-xl font-semibold mb-3">Phone Orders</h3>
 84:               <p className="text-gray-700 dark:text-gray-300">
 85:                 A simple phone call to place your order helps restaurants avoid commission fees completely and provides a personal touch.
 86:               </p>
 87:             </div>
 88:             <div className="bg-white dark:bg-gray-800 p-6 rounded-lg shadow-md border border-gray-200 dark:border-gray-700">
 89:               <h3 className="text-xl font-semibold mb-3">Pickup Options</h3>
 90:               <p className="text-gray-700 dark:text-gray-300">
 91:                 If you're able to pick up your order, this is the most cost-effective option for both you and the restaurant.
 92:               </p>
 93:             </div>
 94:           </div>
 95:         </ContentSection>
 96:         <section className="py-16 bg-wolt-blue text-white">
 97:           <div className="container mx-auto px-4 text-center">
 98:             <h2 className="text-3xl md:text-4xl font-bold mb-8">Join the Movement</h2>
 99:             <p className="text-xl mb-8 max-w-3xl mx-auto">
100:               Every order placed through fairer alternatives sends a message that Danish consumers value ethical business practices.
101:             </p>
102:             <Link 
103:               href="/pledge"
104:               className="inline-block px-8 py-4 bg-white text-wolt-blue hover:bg-gray-100 rounded-lg font-bold text-lg transition duration-200"
105:             >
106:               Sign the Pledge
107:             </Link>
108:           </div>
109:         </section>
110:       </main>
111:     </>
112:   );
113: }

================
File: src/app/contact/page.tsx
================
  1: 'use client';
  2: import { useState } from 'react';
  3: import PageHero from '@/components/PageHero';
  4: import ContentSection from '@/components/ContentSection';
  5: export default function ContactPage() {
  6:   const [formData, setFormData] = useState({
  7:     name: '',
  8:     email: '',
  9:     subject: 'General Inquiry',
 10:     message: '',
 11:   });
 12:   const [isSubmitting, setIsSubmitting] = useState(false);
 13:   const [submitMessage, setSubmitMessage] = useState('');
 14:   const handleChange = (e) => {
 15:     const { name, value } = e.target;
 16:     setFormData(prev => ({
 17:       ...prev,
 18:       [name]: value
 19:     }));
 20:   };
 21:   const handleSubmit = (e) => {
 22:     e.preventDefault();
 23:     setIsSubmitting(true);
 24:     // Placeholder for API call
 25:     setTimeout(() => {
 26:       console.log('Contact form submitted:', formData);
 27:       setSubmitMessage('Thank you for your message! We will get back to you soon.');
 28:       setIsSubmitting(false);
 29:       // Reset form after successful submission
 30:       setFormData({
 31:         name: '',
 32:         email: '',
 33:         subject: 'General Inquiry',
 34:         message: '',
 35:       });
 36:     }, 1500);
 37:   };
 38:   return (
 39:     <>
 40:       <PageHero 
 41:         title="Contact Us"
 42:         subtitle="Have questions or want to get involved? Reach out to us."
 43:         color="secondary"
 44:       />
 45:       <main>
 46:         <section className="py-12 bg-white dark:bg-gray-900">
 47:           <div className="container mx-auto px-4">
 48:             <div className="max-w-3xl mx-auto">
 49:               <div className="bg-white dark:bg-gray-800 p-8 rounded-lg shadow-md border border-gray-200 dark:border-gray-700">
 50:                 {submitMessage ? (
 51:                   <div className="text-center">
 52:                     <div className="text-green-600 dark:text-green-400 text-3xl mb-4">✓</div>
 53:                     <h2 className="text-2xl font-bold mb-4 text-gray-900 dark:text-white">Message Sent</h2>
 54:                     <p className="text-gray-700 dark:text-gray-300 mb-6">{submitMessage}</p>
 55:                     <button 
 56:                       onClick={() => setSubmitMessage('')}
 57:                       className="px-6 py-3 bg-wolt-blue hover:bg-wolt-dark text-white rounded-lg font-medium transition duration-200"
 58:                     >
 59:                       Send Another Message
 60:                     </button>
 61:                   </div>
 62:                 ) : (
 63:                   <>
 64:                     <h2 className="text-2xl font-bold mb-6 text-center text-gray-900 dark:text-white">Get in Touch</h2>
 65:                     <form onSubmit={handleSubmit}>
 66:                       <div className="mb-4">
 67:                         <label htmlFor="name" className="block mb-2 text-gray-700 dark:text-gray-300">Name</label>
 68:                         <input
 69:                           type="text"
 70:                           id="name"
 71:                           name="name"
 72:                           value={formData.name}
 73:                           onChange={handleChange}
 74:                           required
 75:                           className="w-full px-4 py-2 rounded-lg border border-gray-300 dark:border-gray-700 bg-white dark:bg-gray-800 text-gray-900 dark:text-white"
 76:                         />
 77:                       </div>
 78:                       <div className="mb-4">
 79:                         <label htmlFor="email" className="block mb-2 text-gray-700 dark:text-gray-300">Email</label>
 80:                         <input
 81:                           type="email"
 82:                           id="email"
 83:                           name="email"
 84:                           value={formData.email}
 85:                           onChange={handleChange}
 86:                           required
 87:                           className="w-full px-4 py-2 rounded-lg border border-gray-300 dark:border-gray-700 bg-white dark:bg-gray-800 text-gray-900 dark:text-white"
 88:                         />
 89:                       </div>
 90:                       <div className="mb-4">
 91:                         <label htmlFor="subject" className="block mb-2 text-gray-700 dark:text-gray-300">Subject</label>
 92:                         <select
 93:                           id="subject"
 94:                           name="subject"
 95:                           value={formData.subject}
 96:                           onChange={handleChange}
 97:                           className="w-full px-4 py-2 rounded-lg border border-gray-300 dark:border-gray-700 bg-white dark:bg-gray-800 text-gray-900 dark:text-white"
 98:                         >
 99:                           <option value="General Inquiry">General Inquiry</option>
100:                           <option value="Media Request">Media Request</option>
101:                           <option value="Partnership Opportunity">Partnership Opportunity</option>
102:                           <option value="Share My Story">Share My Story</option>
103:                           <option value="Technical Issue">Technical Issue</option>
104:                         </select>
105:                       </div>
106:                       <div className="mb-6">
107:                         <label htmlFor="message" className="block mb-2 text-gray-700 dark:text-gray-300">Message</label>
108:                         <textarea
109:                           id="message"
110:                           name="message"
111:                           value={formData.message}
112:                           onChange={handleChange}
113:                           required
114:                           rows={5}
115:                           className="w-full px-4 py-2 rounded-lg border border-gray-300 dark:border-gray-700 bg-white dark:bg-gray-800 text-gray-900 dark:text-white"
116:                         />
117:                       </div>
118:                       <div className="text-center">
119:                         <button 
120:                           type="submit" 
121:                           disabled={isSubmitting}
122:                           className={`px-6 py-3 bg-wolt-blue hover:bg-wolt-dark text-white rounded-lg font-medium transition duration-200 ${isSubmitting ? 'opacity-70 cursor-not-allowed' : ''}`}
123:                         >
124:                           {isSubmitting ? 'Sending...' : 'Send Message'}
125:                         </button>
126:                       </div>
127:                     </form>
128:                   </>
129:                 )}
130:               </div>
131:             </div>
132:           </div>
133:         </section>
134:         <ContentSection 
135:           title="For Media Inquiries"
136:           bgColor="light"
137:         >
138:           <div className="mb-6">
139:             <p className="mb-4">
140:               We welcome media coverage of the Cancel Wolt movement. Our team can provide:
141:             </p>
142:             <ul className="list-disc pl-6 mb-6 space-y-2">
143:               <li>Statistics and data on Wolt's market practices</li>
144:               <li>Interviews with restaurant owners affected by high commission rates</li>
145:               <li>Stories from delivery workers about their working conditions</li>
146:               <li>Expert commentary on the gig economy and food delivery market</li>
147:             </ul>
148:             <p>
149:               For urgent media requests, please email <a href="mailto:media@cancelwolt.dk" className="text-wolt-blue hover:underline">media@cancelwolt.dk</a> or select "Media Request" in the contact form above.
150:             </p>
151:           </div>
152:         </ContentSection>
153:         <ContentSection 
154:           title="Partnership Opportunities"
155:           bgColor="white"
156:         >
157:           <div className="mb-6">
158:             <p className="mb-4">
159:               We're looking to partner with:
160:             </p>
161:             <div className="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 gap-6 mb-6">
162:               <div className="bg-white dark:bg-gray-800 p-6 rounded-lg shadow-sm border border-gray-200 dark:border-gray-700">
163:                 <h3 className="text-xl font-semibold mb-3">Restaurant Associations</h3>
164:                 <p className="text-gray-700 dark:text-gray-300">
165:                   Join our coalition to advocate for fair commission rates and transparent practices in food delivery.
166:                 </p>
167:               </div>
168:               <div className="bg-white dark:bg-gray-800 p-6 rounded-lg shadow-sm border border-gray-200 dark:border-gray-700">
169:                 <h3 className="text-xl font-semibold mb-3">Labor Unions</h3>
170:                 <p className="text-gray-700 dark:text-gray-300">
171:                   Partner with us to support better working conditions and protections for delivery workers.
172:                 </p>
173:               </div>
174:               <div className="bg-white dark:bg-gray-800 p-6 rounded-lg shadow-sm border border-gray-200 dark:border-gray-700">
175:                 <h3 className="text-xl font-semibold mb-3">Consumer Organizations</h3>
176:                 <p className="text-gray-700 dark:text-gray-300">
177:                   Collaborate on education campaigns about hidden fees and fair alternatives in food delivery.
178:                 </p>
179:               </div>
180:               <div className="bg-white dark:bg-gray-800 p-6 rounded-lg shadow-sm border border-gray-200 dark:border-gray-700">
181:                 <h3 className="text-xl font-semibold mb-3">Local Businesses</h3>
182:                 <p className="text-gray-700 dark:text-gray-300">
183:                   Showcase your restaurant as a "direct ordering" alternative and share your Wolt experience.
184:                 </p>
185:               </div>
186:             </div>
187:             <p>
188:               To discuss partnership opportunities, please select "Partnership Opportunity" in the contact form above.
189:             </p>
190:           </div>
191:         </ContentSection>
192:         <ContentSection 
193:           title="Frequently Asked Questions"
194:           bgColor="light"
195:         >
196:           <div className="space-y-6">
197:             <div>
198:               <h3 className="text-xl font-semibold mb-2">Is this campaign affiliated with any competitors?</h3>
199:               <p className="text-gray-700 dark:text-gray-300">
200:                 No, we are an independent, grassroots movement of concerned consumers, restaurant owners, and delivery workers. We receive no funding from competitors like Just Eat or others.
201:               </p>
202:             </div>
203:             <div>
204:               <h3 className="text-xl font-semibold mb-2">How can I contribute beyond signing the pledge?</h3>
205:               <p className="text-gray-700 dark:text-gray-300">
206:                 You can help by spreading the word, sharing your experiences, volunteering your skills (design, development, writing), or donating to help cover our minimal operating costs.
207:               </p>
208:             </div>
209:             <div>
210:               <h3 className="text-xl font-semibold mb-2">How do you use the data collected in the pledge?</h3>
211:               <p className="text-gray-700 dark:text-gray-300">
212:                 We only use your information to count pledge signatures and to send you occasional updates about the movement. We never sell or share your personal data with third parties. See our Privacy Policy for details.
213:               </p>
214:             </div>
215:             <div>
216:               <h3 className="text-xl font-semibold mb-2">I'm a restaurant owner who wants to share my story. How can I do that?</h3>
217:               <p className="text-gray-700 dark:text-gray-300">
218:                 We'd love to hear from you! Please select "Share My Story" in the contact form above, and someone from our team will follow up to arrange an interview.
219:               </p>
220:             </div>
221:           </div>
222:         </ContentSection>
223:       </main>
224:     </>
225:   );
226: }

================
File: src/app/pledge/page.tsx
================
  1: 'use client';
  2: import { useState } from 'react';
  3: import PageHero from '@/components/PageHero';
  4: import ContentSection from '@/components/ContentSection';
  5: import PledgeForm from '@/components/PledgeForm';
  6: import Link from 'next/link';
  7: import WoltButton from '@/components/WoltButton';
  8: export default function PledgePage() {
  9:   const [submitted, setSubmitted] = useState(false);
 10:   const handleSuccess = () => {
 11:     setSubmitted(true);
 12:     window.scrollTo(0, 0);
 13:   };
 14:   return (
 15:     <>
 16:       <PageHero
 17:         title={submitted ? "Thank You!" : "Sign the Pledge"}
 18:         subtitle={submitted ? "We've sent a verification email. Check your inbox!" : "Join the movement against Wolt's unfair practices"}
 19:         color="primary"
 20:       />
 21:       <main>
 22:         <ContentSection>
 23:           <div className="max-w-4xl mx-auto py-12">
 24:             {submitted ? (
 25:               <div className="text-center">
 26:                 <div className="mb-8 p-6 bg-green-50 dark:bg-green-900 rounded-lg shadow-md">
 27:                   <h2 className="text-2xl font-bold mb-4 text-gray-800 dark:text-gray-100">
 28:                     Your pledge has been submitted!
 29:                   </h2>
 30:                   <p className="mb-4 text-gray-600 dark:text-gray-300">
 31:                     Please check your email for a verification link to confirm your pledge.
 32:                   </p>
 33:                   <p className="text-gray-600 dark:text-gray-300">
 34:                     If you don't see it, check your spam folder. The verification email is required to count your pledge.
 35:                   </p>
 36:                 </div>
 37:                 <div className="space-y-6">
 38:                   <h3 className="text-xl font-semibold text-gray-800 dark:text-gray-100">
 39:                     What's Next?
 40:                   </h3>
 41:                   <div className="space-y-4">
 42:                     <p className="text-gray-600 dark:text-gray-300">
 43:                       While waiting for the verification email, explore some alternatives to Wolt or learn why this movement matters:
 44:                     </p>
 45:                     <div className="flex flex-wrap justify-center gap-4 mt-6">
 46:                       <Link href="/alternatives" passHref>
 47:                         <WoltButton variant="secondary">
 48:                           Explore Alternatives
 49:                         </WoltButton>
 50:                       </Link>
 51:                       <Link href="/restaurants" passHref>
 52:                         <WoltButton variant="secondary">
 53:                           How Restaurants Suffer
 54:                         </WoltButton>
 55:                       </Link>
 56:                       <Link href="/workers" passHref>
 57:                         <WoltButton variant="secondary">
 58:                           Worker Exploitation
 59:                         </WoltButton>
 60:                       </Link>
 61:                     </div>
 62:                   </div>
 63:                 </div>
 64:               </div>
 65:             ) : (
 66:               <div className="grid md:grid-cols-5 gap-8">
 67:                 <div className="md:col-span-3">
 68:                   <h2 className="text-2xl font-bold mb-4 text-gray-800 dark:text-gray-100">
 69:                     Why Your Pledge Matters
 70:                   </h2>
 71:                   <div className="prose dark:prose-invert max-w-none">
 72:                     <p>
 73:                       By signing this pledge, you're taking a stand against Wolt's business practices that:
 74:                     </p>
 75:                     <ul className="space-y-2">
 76:                       <li>Charge restaurants up to 30% commissions, squeezing their already thin margins</li>
 77:                       <li>Add hidden "service fees" to customers while still charging for delivery</li>
 78:                       <li>Treat workers as contractors, denying them employment benefits</li>
 79:                       <li>Create a monopolistic market that hurts local businesses</li>
 80:                     </ul>
 81:                     <p className="mt-4 font-semibold">
 82:                       Your pledge will:
 83:                     </p>
 84:                     <ol className="space-y-2">
 85:                       <li>Be counted in our public tally of Wolt resisters</li>
 86:                       <li>Help demonstrate consumer demand for fairer business practices</li>
 87:                       <li>Add strength to our movement for a more ethical food delivery ecosystem</li>
 88:                     </ol>
 89:                     <p className="mt-4 text-sm text-gray-500">
 90:                       Note: Your email address will only be used to verify your pledge and will not be shared with third parties.
 91:                     </p>
 92:                   </div>
 93:                 </div>
 94:                 <div className="md:col-span-2">
 95:                   <PledgeForm onSuccess={handleSuccess} />
 96:                 </div>
 97:               </div>
 98:             )}
 99:           </div>
100:         </ContentSection>
101:       </main>
102:     </>
103:   );
104: }

================
File: src/app/pricing/page.tsx
================
  1: 'use client';
  2: import PageHero from '@/components/PageHero';
  3: import ContentSection from '@/components/ContentSection';
  4: import Citation from '@/components/Citation';
  5: import ComparisonTable from '@/components/ComparisonTable';
  6: import Link from 'next/link';
  7: export default function PricingPage() {
  8:   // Comparison table data
  9:   const feeComparisonData = [
 10:     {
 11:       id: 'commission',
 12:       label: 'Restaurant Commission',
 13:       wolt: '25-30%',
 14:       justEat: '10-15%',
 15:       direct: '0%',
 16:       woltHighlight: true,
 17:       justEatHighlight: false,
 18:       directHighlight: false
 19:     },
 20:     {
 21:       id: 'service-fee',
 22:       label: 'Service Fee (Customer)',
 23:       wolt: '5%',
 24:       justEat: '0%',
 25:       direct: '0%',
 26:       woltHighlight: true,
 27:       justEatHighlight: false,
 28:       directHighlight: false
 29:     },
 30:     {
 31:       id: 'delivery-fee',
 32:       label: 'Delivery Fee',
 33:       wolt: '29-59 kr',
 34:       justEat: '29-49 kr',
 35:       direct: 'Varies',
 36:       woltHighlight: true,
 37:       justEatHighlight: false,
 38:       directHighlight: false
 39:     },
 40:     {
 41:       id: 'small-order',
 42:       label: 'Small Order Fee',
 43:       wolt: '10-25 kr',
 44:       justEat: '0-15 kr',
 45:       direct: '0 kr',
 46:       woltHighlight: true,
 47:       justEatHighlight: false,
 48:       directHighlight: false
 49:     },
 50:     {
 51:       id: 'price-parity',
 52:       label: 'Price Parity Clause',
 53:       wolt: 'Yes',
 54:       justEat: 'No',
 55:       direct: 'N/A',
 56:       woltHighlight: true,
 57:       justEatHighlight: false,
 58:       directHighlight: false
 59:     },
 60:     {
 61:       id: 'menu-price',
 62:       label: 'Menu Price Increase',
 63:       wolt: '15-25%',
 64:       justEat: '0-10%',
 65:       direct: '0%',
 66:       woltHighlight: true,
 67:       justEatHighlight: false,
 68:       directHighlight: false
 69:     }
 70:   ];
 71:   // Example cost breakdown for a 200 DKK order
 72:   const orderComparisonData = [
 73:     {
 74:       id: 'base-price',
 75:       label: 'Base Food Price',
 76:       wolt: 155,
 77:       justEat: 170,
 78:       direct: 155,
 79:       unit: ' kr'
 80:     },
 81:     {
 82:       id: 'menu-markup',
 83:       label: 'Menu Price Markup',
 84:       wolt: 45,
 85:       justEat: 20,
 86:       direct: 0,
 87:       unit: ' kr',
 88:       woltHighlight: true
 89:     },
 90:     {
 91:       id: 'delivery-fee',
 92:       label: 'Delivery Fee',
 93:       wolt: 39,
 94:       justEat: 29,
 95:       direct: 0,
 96:       unit: ' kr',
 97:       woltHighlight: true
 98:     },
 99:     {
100:       id: 'service-fee',
101:       label: 'Service Fee',
102:       wolt: 10,
103:       justEat: 0,
104:       direct: 0,
105:       unit: ' kr',
106:       woltHighlight: true
107:     },
108:     {
109:       id: 'total',
110:       label: 'Total You Pay',
111:       wolt: 249,
112:       justEat: 219,
113:       direct: 155,
114:       unit: ' kr',
115:       woltHighlight: true,
116:       directHighlight: false
117:     }
118:   ];
119:   return (
120:     <>
121:       <PageHero 
122:         title="Overpriced & Unfair Pricing"
123:         subtitle="How Wolt's fee structure hurts consumers and restaurants"
124:         color="primary"
125:         imageUrl="/images/unfair_pricing.png"
126:       />
127:       <main>
128:         <ContentSection 
129:           title="Hidden Fees You're Paying"
130:           bgColor="white"
131:           imageUrl="/images/hidden_cost.png"
132:           imageAlt="Hidden costs illustration showing increased prices"
133:         >
134:           <p className="mb-4">
135:             When you order through Wolt, the price you pay is significantly higher than ordering directly from the restaurant due to multiple layers of fees:
136:           </p>
137:           <ul className="list-disc pl-6 mb-6 space-y-3">
138:             <li>
139:               <strong>Service Fee:</strong> Wolt adds an additional ~5% "service fee" on every order, even if you already pay for Wolt Plus.<Citation id="1" source="Wolt Fee Structure Analysis, 2023" /> This is on top of the delivery fee you're already paying.
140:             </li>
141:             <li>
142:               <strong>Inflated Menu Prices:</strong> Restaurants must increase their menu prices on Wolt by ~20–30% just to survive the high commissions.<Citation id="2" source="Restaurant Pricing Study, Copenhagen, 2023" /> This means you're paying more for the exact same meal than if you ordered directly.
143:             </li>
144:             <li>
145:               <strong>Delivery Fee:</strong> The standard fee for delivery, which varies by distance but typically starts at 29-39 DKK.
146:             </li>
147:             <li>
148:               <strong>Small Order Fee:</strong> An additional fee for orders below a certain amount, typically 120-150 DKK.
149:             </li>
150:           </ul>
151:           <div className="bg-gray-100 dark:bg-gray-800 p-4 rounded-lg mb-6">
152:             <h3 className="font-semibold mb-2">Example: 200 DKK Order</h3>
153:             <p className="mb-2">Here's how a typical 200 DKK order breaks down:</p>
154:             <ul className="space-y-1">
155:               <li>Original restaurant price: ~155 DKK</li>
156:               <li>Wolt markup: ~45 DKK (29% increase)</li>
157:               <li>Delivery fee: 39 DKK</li>
158:               <li>Service fee: ~10 DKK (5% of order)</li>
159:               <li><strong>Total you pay: 249 DKK</strong></li>
160:               <li>Total if ordered directly: 155 DKK (60% cheaper)</li>
161:             </ul>
162:           </div>
163:           <p>
164:             Even with a Wolt+ subscription (which costs 49 DKK/month), you're still paying the service fee and inflated menu prices.
165:           </p>
166:         </ContentSection>
167:         <ContentSection 
168:           title="Fee Comparison: Wolt vs Alternatives"
169:           bgColor="light"
170:           imageUrl="/images/hurting_restaurants.png"
171:           imageAlt="Restaurant owner impacted by high delivery fees"
172:         >
173:           <p className="mb-6">
174:             This table compares the fees and policies across different food ordering options, showing how much more expensive Wolt is compared to alternatives:
175:           </p>
176:           <ComparisonTable 
177:             title="Fee Structure Comparison"
178:             items={feeComparisonData}
179:             colorScheme="fees"
180:           />
181:           <ComparisonTable 
182:             title="Real Cost Example: 200 kr Order"
183:             description="How the same meal would cost across different platforms"
184:             items={orderComparisonData}
185:             colorScheme="fees"
186:           />
187:           <p className="mt-6">
188:             The price differences are substantial. For a typical order, you could save 30-40% by ordering directly from the restaurant instead of using Wolt.
189:           </p>
190:         </ContentSection>
191:         <ContentSection 
192:           title="Anti-Competitive Price Parity Clauses"
193:           bgColor="white"
194:         >
195:           <p className="mb-4">
196:             Wolt has enforced a rule that restaurants can't offer lower prices in-store or on other platforms than on Wolt.<Citation id="3" source="Danish Competition Authority Investigation, 2023" /> This anti-competitive clause (now under legal challenge in Denmark) forces prices up across the board and prevents you from getting a better deal by ordering directly.
197:           </p>
198:           <p className="mb-4">
199:             In Finland, regulators already forced Wolt to drop this clause<Citation id="4" source="Finnish Competition Authority Ruling, 2022" /> after determining it was harmful to market competition.
200:           </p>
201:           <div className="bg-yellow-50 dark:bg-yellow-900 p-4 rounded-lg border-l-4 border-yellow-500 my-6">
202:             <h3 className="font-semibold text-yellow-900 dark:text-yellow-100 mb-2">What This Means For You</h3>
203:             <p className="text-yellow-800 dark:text-yellow-200">
204:               Because of these price parity clauses, even restaurants that want to offer you better prices when you order directly can't do so explicitly. This artificially keeps prices high across all channels.
205:             </p>
206:           </div>
207:           <p>
208:             Competitors like Just Eat take a smaller commission from restaurants—around 10–15% for orders where restaurants handle delivery themselves<Citation id="5" source="Just Eat Partner Program, Denmark, 2023" />—significantly less than Wolt's ~30%.
209:           </p>
210:         </ContentSection>
211:         <section className="py-16 bg-wolt-blue text-white">
212:           <div className="container mx-auto px-4 text-center">
213:             <h2 className="text-3xl md:text-4xl font-bold mb-8">Stop Paying Hidden Fees</h2>
214:             <p className="text-xl mb-8 max-w-3xl mx-auto">
215:               By choosing alternatives to Wolt, you not only save money but also support fair practices in the food delivery ecosystem.
216:             </p>
217:             <div className="flex flex-wrap justify-center gap-4">
218:               <Link 
219:                 href="/alternatives"
220:                 className="inline-block px-6 py-3 bg-white text-wolt-blue hover:bg-gray-100 rounded-lg font-medium transition duration-200"
221:               >
222:                 Find Better Alternatives
223:               </Link>
224:               <Link 
225:                 href="/pledge"
226:                 className="inline-block px-6 py-3 bg-transparent border border-white text-white hover:bg-white/10 rounded-lg font-medium transition duration-200"
227:               >
228:                 Sign the Pledge
229:               </Link>
230:             </div>
231:           </div>
232:         </section>
233:       </main>
234:     </>
235:   );
236: }

================
File: src/app/privacy/page.tsx
================
  1: 'use client';
  2: import PageHero from '@/components/PageHero';
  3: import ContentSection from '@/components/ContentSection';
  4: export default function PrivacyPage() {
  5:   return (
  6:     <>
  7:       <PageHero 
  8:         title="Privacy Policy"
  9:         subtitle="How we collect, use, and protect your information"
 10:         color="secondary"
 11:       />
 12:       <main>
 13:         <ContentSection 
 14:           title="Overview"
 15:           bgColor="white"
 16:         >
 17:           <p className="mb-4">
 18:             This Privacy Policy explains how Cancel Wolt ("we", "us", or "our") collects, uses, and 
 19:             shares your personal information through our website cancelwolt.dk. We are committed to
 20:             protecting your privacy and handling your data in an open and transparent manner.
 21:           </p>
 22:           <p className="mb-4">
 23:             This policy was last updated on June 1, 2024 and complies with the General Data Protection
 24:             Regulation (GDPR).
 25:           </p>
 26:         </ContentSection>
 27:         <ContentSection 
 28:           title="What Information We Collect"
 29:           bgColor="light"
 30:         >
 31:           <p className="mb-4">
 32:             We collect the following types of information:
 33:           </p>
 34:           <h3 className="text-xl font-semibold mb-2">Information You Provide to Us</h3>
 35:           <ul className="list-disc pl-6 mb-6 space-y-2">
 36:             <li>
 37:               <strong>Pledge Form:</strong> When you sign our pledge, we collect your name, email address, 
 38:               city (optional), and any comments you provide about why you're joining the movement.
 39:             </li>
 40:             <li>
 41:               <strong>Contact Form:</strong> When you contact us, we collect your name, email address, 
 42:               the subject of your inquiry, and your message.
 43:             </li>
 44:           </ul>
 45:           <h3 className="text-xl font-semibold mb-2">Information We Collect Automatically</h3>
 46:           <ul className="list-disc pl-6 mb-6 space-y-2">
 47:             <li>
 48:               <strong>Usage Data:</strong> We use Plausible Analytics, a privacy-friendly analytics tool
 49:               that collects anonymous usage statistics. This includes page views, referral sources, and
 50:               browser information, but does not use cookies or collect personal data.
 51:             </li>
 52:           </ul>
 53:         </ContentSection>
 54:         <ContentSection 
 55:           title="How We Use Your Information"
 56:           bgColor="white"
 57:         >
 58:           <p className="mb-4">
 59:             We use the information we collect for the following purposes:
 60:           </p>
 61:           <ul className="list-disc pl-6 mb-6 space-y-3">
 62:             <li>
 63:               <strong>To Process Your Pledge:</strong> We use your name and email to register your 
 64:               pledge and send you a confirmation email.
 65:             </li>
 66:             <li>
 67:               <strong>To Communicate With You:</strong> We use your contact information to respond 
 68:               to your inquiries and send occasional updates about the movement (you can opt out of these 
 69:               at any time).
 70:             </li>
 71:             <li>
 72:               <strong>To Improve Our Website:</strong> We use anonymous usage data to understand how 
 73:               visitors interact with our site and make improvements.
 74:             </li>
 75:             <li>
 76:               <strong>To Count Pledges:</strong> We use anonymous counts of pledges to display the 
 77:               total number of supporters on our website.
 78:             </li>
 79:           </ul>
 80:           <p className="mb-4">
 81:             <strong>Legal Basis for Processing:</strong> We process your data based on the following 
 82:             legal grounds:
 83:           </p>
 84:           <ul className="list-disc pl-6 mb-6 space-y-2">
 85:             <li>Your consent (which you can withdraw at any time)</li>
 86:             <li>The legitimate interests of our movement (such as improving our website)</li>
 87:             <li>To fulfill our contractual obligations to you when you sign the pledge</li>
 88:           </ul>
 89:         </ContentSection>
 90:         <ContentSection 
 91:           title="How We Share Your Information"
 92:           bgColor="light"
 93:         >
 94:           <p className="mb-4">
 95:             We do not sell, rent, or trade your personal information to third parties. We may share your
 96:             information in the following limited circumstances:
 97:           </p>
 98:           <ul className="list-disc pl-6 mb-6 space-y-3">
 99:             <li>
100:               <strong>Service Providers:</strong> We use Supabase to store pledge and contact form data. 
101:               These providers are contractually bound to protect your data and only process it according 
102:               to our instructions.
103:             </li>
104:             <li>
105:               <strong>Aggregated Statistics:</strong> We may disclose anonymized, aggregated statistics
106:               about the number of pledges and their geographic distribution.
107:             </li>
108:             <li>
109:               <strong>Legal Requirements:</strong> We may disclose your information if required by law,
110:               such as in response to a court order or legal process.
111:             </li>
112:           </ul>
113:         </ContentSection>
114:         <ContentSection 
115:           title="Data Retention and Security"
116:           bgColor="white"
117:         >
118:           <h3 className="text-xl font-semibold mb-2">How Long We Keep Your Data</h3>
119:           <p className="mb-4">
120:             We retain your personal data for as long as necessary to fulfill the purposes for which it
121:             was collected, or as required by applicable laws. Specifically:
122:           </p>
123:           <ul className="list-disc pl-6 mb-6 space-y-2">
124:             <li>Pledge data: Retained for the duration of the Cancel Wolt campaign</li>
125:             <li>Contact form submissions: Retained for up to 1 year after resolution</li>
126:           </ul>
127:           <h3 className="text-xl font-semibold mb-2">How We Protect Your Data</h3>
128:           <p className="mb-4">
129:             We implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect your personal data
130:             against unauthorized or unlawful processing, accidental loss, destruction, or damage. These
131:             include:
132:           </p>
133:           <ul className="list-disc pl-6 mb-6 space-y-2">
134:             <li>Secure hosting on Vercel's infrastructure</li>
135:             <li>Encryption of data in transit using HTTPS</li>
136:             <li>Access controls to limit who can access your data</li>
137:             <li>Regular security reviews and updates</li>
138:           </ul>
139:         </ContentSection>
140:         <ContentSection 
141:           title="Your Privacy Rights"
142:           bgColor="light"
143:         >
144:           <p className="mb-4">
145:             Under the GDPR, you have the following rights regarding your personal data:
146:           </p>
147:           <ul className="list-disc pl-6 mb-6 space-y-3">
148:             <li>
149:               <strong>Right to Access:</strong> You can request a copy of the personal data we hold about you.
150:             </li>
151:             <li>
152:               <strong>Right to Rectification:</strong> You can ask us to correct inaccurate or incomplete information.
153:             </li>
154:             <li>
155:               <strong>Right to Erasure:</strong> You can ask us to delete your personal data in certain circumstances.
156:             </li>
157:             <li>
158:               <strong>Right to Restrict Processing:</strong> You can ask us to limit how we use your data.
159:             </li>
160:             <li>
161:               <strong>Right to Data Portability:</strong> You can ask for a copy of your data in a machine-readable format.
162:             </li>
163:             <li>
164:               <strong>Right to Object:</strong> You can object to our processing of your data for certain purposes.
165:             </li>
166:             <li>
167:               <strong>Right to Withdraw Consent:</strong> You can withdraw your consent at any time.
168:             </li>
169:           </ul>
170:           <p className="mb-4">
171:             To exercise any of these rights, please contact us at <a href="mailto:privacy@cancelwolt.dk" className="text-wolt-blue hover:underline">privacy@cancelwolt.dk</a>. 
172:             We will respond to your request within 30 days.
173:           </p>
174:         </ContentSection>
175:         <ContentSection 
176:           title="Cookies and Similar Technologies"
177:           bgColor="white"
178:         >
179:           <p className="mb-4">
180:             Our website uses minimal cookies that are necessary for the website to function properly. 
181:             We do not use cookies for advertising or tracking purposes.
182:           </p>
183:           <p className="mb-4">
184:             The analytics tool we use (Plausible) is cookie-free and does not track individuals across
185:             websites or devices.
186:           </p>
187:           <p className="mb-4">
188:             You can control cookies through your browser settings. Please note that blocking essential
189:             cookies may affect the functionality of our website.
190:           </p>
191:         </ContentSection>
192:         <ContentSection 
193:           title="Changes to This Policy"
194:           bgColor="light"
195:         >
196:           <p className="mb-4">
197:             We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. We will notify you of any changes by posting
198:             the new policy on this page and updating the "last updated" date.
199:           </p>
200:           <p className="mb-4">
201:             We encourage you to review this policy periodically to stay informed about how we protect your
202:             personal information.
203:           </p>
204:         </ContentSection>
205:         <ContentSection 
206:           title="Contact Us"
207:           bgColor="white"
208:         >
209:           <p className="mb-4">
210:             If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy or our data practices, please contact us at:
211:           </p>
212:           <div className="mb-4">
213:             <p>Email: <a href="mailto:privacy@cancelwolt.dk" className="text-wolt-blue hover:underline">privacy@cancelwolt.dk</a></p>
214:           </div>
215:           <p className="mb-4">
216:             You also have the right to lodge a complaint with the Danish Data Protection Agency (Datatilsynet)
217:             if you believe that the processing of your personal data violates applicable laws.
218:           </p>
219:         </ContentSection>
220:       </main>
221:     </>
222:   );
223: }

================
File: src/app/restaurants/page.tsx
================
  1: 'use client';
  2: import PageHero from '@/components/PageHero';
  3: import ContentSection from '@/components/ContentSection';
  4: import Citation from '@/components/Citation';
  5: import Link from 'next/link';
  6: export default function RestaurantsPage() {
  7:   return (
  8:     <>
  9:       <PageHero 
 10:         title="Hurting Local Restaurants"
 11:         subtitle="How Wolt's high commissions and practices squeeze local businesses"
 12:         color="primary"
 13:       />
 14:       <main>
 15:         <ContentSection 
 16:           title="The Commission Squeeze"
 17:           bgColor="white"
 18:         >
 19:           <p className="mb-4">
 20:             Local restaurants are caught in a difficult bind with Wolt. The combination of high commissions and forced price-matching hits small businesses particularly hard:
 21:           </p>
 22:           <ul className="list-disc pl-6 mb-6 space-y-3">
 23:             <li>
 24:               <strong>High Commission Rates:</strong> Wolt charges restaurants 25-30% commission on every order.<Citation id="1" source="Restaurant Association of Denmark Report, 2023" /> For many restaurants, their profit margin is only 3-5% to begin with.
 25:             </li>
 26:             <li>
 27:               <strong>Price Parity Requirements:</strong> Restaurants can't offer lower prices on other platforms or directly to customers, preventing them from recovering the high commission costs through direct sales.<Citation id="2" source="Competition Analysis, Food Delivery Market Denmark, 2023" />
 28:             </li>
 29:             <li>
 30:               <strong>Absorption of Costs:</strong> Many restaurants have to either absorb these high costs (reducing their already thin profit margins) or raise menu prices across all channels.
 31:             </li>
 32:           </ul>
 33:           <div className="bg-red-50 dark:bg-red-900 p-4 rounded-lg border-l-4 border-red-500 my-6">
 34:             <h3 className="font-semibold text-red-900 dark:text-red-100 mb-2">Restaurant Boycott</h3>
 35:             <p className="text-red-800 dark:text-red-200">
 36:               Over 30 restaurants in Aalborg collectively boycotted Wolt in 2022<Citation id="3" source="Aalborg Restaurant Boycott, 2022" /> in protest of these high fees, saying they "can't make ends meet" when Wolt takes such a high cut on every sale.
 37:             </p>
 38:           </div>
 39:           <p>
 40:             One restaurant owner noted, "it's only a matter of time before takeaway shops go under" if these conditions continue.<Citation id="4" source="Restaurant Owner Interview, Danish Food Industry Magazine, 2023" /> The math simply doesn't work for many small businesses.
 41:           </p>
 42:         </ContentSection>
 43:         <ContentSection 
 44:           title="The Dependence Trap"
 45:           bgColor="light"
 46:         >
 47:           <p className="mb-4">
 48:             Wolt's aggressive expansion has created a dependence trap for many restaurants. As consumers increasingly rely on delivery apps for discovering and ordering food, restaurants feel forced to join platforms like Wolt despite the disadvantageous terms.
 49:           </p>
 50:           <p className="mb-4">
 51:             This dynamic creates several issues:
 52:           </p>
 53:           <ul className="list-disc pl-6 mb-6 space-y-2">
 54:             <li>
 55:               <strong>Market Dominance:</strong> In Copenhagen, Wolt has near-monopoly power in the delivery market.<Citation id="5" source="Market Share Analysis, Danish Delivery Apps, 2023" />
 56:             </li>
 57:             <li>
 58:               <strong>Customer Capture:</strong> One Aalborg eatery reported that 90% of its orders came via Wolt,<Citation id="6" source="Small Business Impact Survey, Northern Jutland, 2023" /> making it nearly impossible to leave the platform.
 59:             </li>
 60:             <li>
 61:               <strong>Leverage Imbalance:</strong> This dominance gives Wolt enormous leverage to dictate terms, including the harsh price clauses that prevent restaurants from offering better prices directly.
 62:             </li>
 63:           </ul>
 64:           <p>
 65:             Many small restaurants feel they "can't live with Wolt, but can't live without it"—a classic abusive monopoly scenario.<Citation id="7" source="Economic Analysis of Food Delivery Market, University of Copenhagen, 2023" />
 66:           </p>
 67:         </ContentSection>
 68:         <ContentSection 
 69:           title="Impact on Competition"
 70:           bgColor="white"
 71:         >
 72:           <p className="mb-4">
 73:             Wolt's market power has even helped push out rivals. Foodora (owner of Hungry.dk) tried to challenge Wolt in Denmark by offering lower fees and better service to restaurants, giving restaurants hope for an alternative.<Citation id="8" source="Foodora Denmark Market Entry Analysis, 2022" />
 74:           </p>
 75:           <p className="mb-4">
 76:             But after only about 18 months, Foodora pulled out of Denmark in 2024, citing the tough market dominated by Wolt. With one less competitor, Wolt's grip on restaurants—and its ability to charge high fees—only grows stronger.
 77:           </p>
 78:           <div className="bg-yellow-50 dark:bg-yellow-900 p-4 rounded-lg border-l-4 border-yellow-500 my-6">
 79:             <h3 className="font-semibold text-yellow-900 dark:text-yellow-100 mb-2">The Restaurant Math</h3>
 80:             <p className="text-yellow-800 dark:text-yellow-200 mb-2">
 81:               For a typical €15 meal:
 82:             </p>
 83:             <ul className="space-y-1 text-yellow-800 dark:text-yellow-200">
 84:               <li>€4.50 (30%) goes to Wolt</li>
 85:               <li>€6.00-7.50 goes to food costs</li>
 86:               <li>€2.00-3.00 goes to staff and overhead</li>
 87:               <li>€0.00-2.50 left as profit (often negative)</li>
 88:             </ul>
 89:           </div>
 90:         </ContentSection>
 91:         <ContentSection 
 92:           title="Supporting Local Alternatives"
 93:           bgColor="light"
 94:         >
 95:           <p className="mb-4">
 96:             There are better ways to support your local restaurants:
 97:           </p>
 98:           <ul className="list-disc pl-6 mb-6 space-y-3">
 99:             <li>
100:               <strong>Just Eat:</strong> Remains a viable alternative, as it already works with a huge number of Danish restaurants and often allows "bring-your-own courier" or pickup, which avoids extreme commissions.
101:             </li>
102:             <li>
103:               <strong>Order Direct:</strong> The best alternative is to order directly from the restaurant (by phone or their website) and pick up your food or use their in-house delivery if offered. This way, the restaurant keeps 100% of the payment.
104:             </li>
105:             <li>
106:               <strong>Local Initiatives:</strong> Some Danish municipalities and cooperatives are exploring local delivery platforms to break the stranglehold of apps like Wolt.
107:             </li>
108:           </ul>
109:           <div className="bg-green-50 dark:bg-green-900 p-4 rounded-lg my-6">
110:             <h3 className="font-semibold text-green-900 dark:text-green-100 mb-2">The Impact of Direct Ordering</h3>
111:             <p className="text-green-800 dark:text-green-200">
112:               "When customers order directly from us, we can afford to offer better prices, pay our staff better wages, and stay in business. Every direct order helps us survive." — Restaurant owner, Copenhagen
113:             </p>
114:           </div>
115:           <p>
116:             Remember: every time you avoid Wolt's fees, you're putting that money back into your local community and workers' pockets, not a tech platform's coffers.
117:           </p>
118:         </ContentSection>
119:         <section className="py-16 bg-wolt-blue text-white">
120:           <div className="container mx-auto px-4 text-center">
121:             <h2 className="text-3xl md:text-4xl font-bold mb-8">Support Your Local Restaurants</h2>
122:             <p className="text-xl mb-8 max-w-3xl mx-auto">
123:               By ordering directly or using fairer delivery options, you help local restaurants thrive and keep money in your community.
124:             </p>
125:             <div className="flex flex-wrap justify-center gap-4">
126:               <Link 
127:                 href="/alternatives"
128:                 className="inline-block px-6 py-3 bg-white text-wolt-blue hover:bg-gray-100 rounded-lg font-medium transition duration-200"
129:               >
130:                 Find Direct Ordering Options
131:               </Link>
132:               <Link 
133:                 href="/pledge"
134:                 className="inline-block px-6 py-3 bg-transparent border border-white text-white hover:bg-white/10 rounded-lg font-medium transition duration-200"
135:               >
136:                 Sign the Pledge
137:               </Link>
138:             </div>
139:           </div>
140:         </section>
141:       </main>
142:     </>
143:   );
144: }

================
File: src/app/success-stories/page.tsx
================
  1: 'use client';
  2: import { motion } from 'framer-motion';
  3: import PageHero from '@/components/PageHero';
  4: import ContentSection from '@/components/ContentSection';
  5: import Citation from '@/components/Citation';
  6: import Link from 'next/link';
  7: export default function SuccessStoriesPage() {
  8:   return (
  9:     <>
 10:       <PageHero 
 11:         title="Success Stories"
 12:         subtitle="Restaurants thriving after leaving Wolt behind"
 13:         color="primary"
 14:       />
 15:       <main>
 16:         <section className="py-12 bg-white dark:bg-gray-900">
 17:           <div className="container mx-auto px-4">
 18:             <div className="max-w-4xl mx-auto">
 19:               <motion.p 
 20:                 initial={{ opacity: 0 }}
 21:                 animate={{ opacity: 1 }}
 22:                 transition={{ duration: 0.8 }}
 23:                 className="text-xl text-center text-gray-700 dark:text-gray-300 mb-12"
 24:               >
 25:                 These restaurants took control of their business by reducing or eliminating their dependence on Wolt. 
 26:                 Read their stories and see how they're thriving with more sustainable alternatives.
 27:               </motion.p>
 28:             </div>
 29:           </div>
 30:         </section>
 31:         <ContentSection 
 32:           title="Café Nord: 40% Profit Increase After Going Direct"
 33:           bgColor="light"
 34:           imageUrl="/images/success-1.jpg"
 35:           imageAlt="Café Nord in Copenhagen"
 36:         >
 37:           <p className="mb-4">
 38:             When Erik Jensen opened Café Nord in Nørrebro in 2019, he initially signed up with Wolt to boost visibility. 
 39:             But after calculating the true cost of their 28% commission, he realized he was losing money on most Wolt orders.
 40:           </p>
 41:           <p className="mb-4">
 42:             "After paying food costs, staff, and Wolt's commission, I was making less than 1% profit on Wolt orders. 
 43:             Sometimes we were actually losing money," says Erik.<Citation id="1" source="Interview with Erik Jensen, Café Nord, 2023" />
 44:           </p>
 45:           <p className="mb-4">
 46:             In January 2023, Erik launched his own online ordering system through his website and began offering a 
 47:             15% discount for direct pickup orders. He invested in local marketing and an email list.
 48:           </p>
 49:           <div className="bg-white dark:bg-gray-800 p-4 rounded-lg my-6">
 50:             <h3 className="font-semibold mb-2">Results:</h3>
 51:             <ul className="list-disc pl-6 space-y-1">
 52:               <li>40% increase in profit margin on takeaway orders</li>
 53:               <li>Built a loyal customer base who order directly</li>
 54:               <li>Complete control over customer data and marketing</li>
 55:               <li>Reduced menu prices while increasing profits</li>
 56:             </ul>
 57:           </div>
 58:           <p>
 59:             "Our customers are happier knowing their money goes to us, not a middleman. Many have told us they
