 60:             appreciate the lower prices and prefer supporting us directly," Erik adds.
 61:           </p>
 62:         </ContentSection>
 63:         <ContentSection 
 64:           title="Aalborg Restaurant Collective: Strength in Numbers"
 65:           bgColor="white"
 66:           imageUrl="/images/success-2.jpg"
 67:           imageAlt="Aalborg restaurant owners meeting"
 68:         >
 69:           <p className="mb-4">
 70:             In 2022, over 30 restaurants in Aalborg made headlines when they collectively decided to leave 
 71:             Wolt in protest of high commission rates.<Citation id="2" source="Aalborg Restaurant Boycott Report, 2022" />
 72:           </p>
 73:           <p className="mb-4">
 74:             Led by Maria Nielsen of Pizza Pronto, the group formed the "Aalborg Direct" initiative, pooling 
 75:             resources to create a shared marketing campaign encouraging customers to order directly from restaurants.
 76:           </p>
 77:           <p className="mb-4">
 78:             "Initially we were worried about losing customers," says Maria. "But we found that with a united front, 
 79:             we could educate customers about why direct ordering is better for everyone. The response was incredible."
 80:           </p>
 81:           <div className="bg-white dark:bg-gray-800 p-4 rounded-lg my-6">
 82:             <h3 className="font-semibold mb-2">Collective Results:</h3>
 83:             <ul className="list-disc pl-6 space-y-1">
 84:               <li>Developed a shared local marketing strategy</li>
 85:               <li>Created a simple website listing all participating restaurants</li>
 86:               <li>Shared costs for promotional materials and local advertising</li>
 87:               <li>Several restaurants reported 20-30% higher revenue after the transition</li>
 88:             </ul>
 89:           </div>
 90:           <p>
 91:             The initiative gained so much local support that even restaurants that initially stayed with Wolt 
 92:             eventually joined the collective. Today, "Aalborg Direct" has become a model for other cities.
 93:           </p>
 94:         </ContentSection>
 95:         <ContentSection 
 96:           title="Spice of India: Building Their Own Delivery Team"
 97:           bgColor="light"
 98:           imageUrl="/images/success-3.jpg"
 99:           imageAlt="Spice of India restaurant delivery staff"
100:         >
101:           <p className="mb-4">
102:             For Raj Patel, owner of Spice of India in Copenhagen, the pandemic initially drove him to rely heavily 
103:             on Wolt. But as delivery became a permanent part of his business model, the economics became unsustainable.
104:           </p>
105:           <p className="mb-4">
106:             "At one point, nearly 70% of our orders were coming through Wolt, and we were paying them over 250,000 DKK 
107:             in commissions annually," Raj explains.<Citation id="3" source="Copenhagen Restaurant Economic Impact Study, 2023" />
108:           </p>
109:           <p className="mb-4">
110:             In mid-2023, Raj made a bold move: he hired two full-time delivery drivers, launched his own website with online 
111:             ordering, and gradually reduced his reliance on Wolt.
112:           </p>
113:           <div className="bg-white dark:bg-gray-800 p-4 rounded-lg my-6">
114:             <h3 className="font-semibold mb-2">Results:</h3>
115:             <ul className="list-disc pl-6 space-y-1">
116:               <li>Created two full-time jobs with proper benefits and protections</li>
117:               <li>Reduced delivery times from 45-60 minutes (Wolt) to 25-35 minutes (in-house)</li>
118:               <li>Maintained control over food quality and presentation</li>
119:               <li>Saved approximately 180,000 DKK in annual commission fees</li>
120:             </ul>
121:           </div>
122:           <p>
123:             "Our drivers know our customers by name now. They're part of our team and care about the service they provide. 
124:             It's a completely different experience than having random couriers who have no connection to our restaurant," says Raj.
125:           </p>
126:         </ContentSection>
127:         <ContentSection 
128:           title="Café Vesterbro: Partnering with Just Eat"
129:           bgColor="white"
130:           imageUrl="/images/success-4.jpg"
131:           imageAlt="Café Vesterbro storefront"
132:         >
133:           <p className="mb-4">
134:             Not every restaurant has the resources to go completely independent. For Luna Hansen at Café Vesterbro, 
135:             switching from Wolt to Just Eat offered a middle ground with substantial benefits.
136:           </p>
137:           <p className="mb-4">
138:             "We were paying Wolt nearly 30% commission. Just Eat offered us their self-delivery option with only 
139:             12% commission for orders where we handle delivery ourselves," Luna explains.<Citation id="4" source="Just Eat Partnership Case Study, 2023" />
140:           </p>
141:           <p className="mb-4">
142:             Luna negotiated with Just Eat to maintain control over pricing and worked to drive customers to order pickup 
143:             directly whenever possible.
144:           </p>
145:           <div className="bg-white dark:bg-gray-800 p-4 rounded-lg my-6">
146:             <h3 className="font-semibold mb-2">Results:</h3>
147:             <ul className="list-disc pl-6 space-y-1">
148:               <li>Over 60% reduction in commission fees</li>
149:               <li>Maintained visibility on a major platform</li>
150:               <li>Greater flexibility in menu pricing and special offers</li>
151:               <li>Just Eat's collective agreement with 3F means better conditions for delivery workers</li>
152:             </ul>
153:           </div>
154:           <p>
155:             "I still encourage customers to order direct when possible, but for those who prefer using an app, Just Eat 
156:             has been a much more sustainable partner for our business," says Luna.
157:           </p>
158:         </ContentSection>
159:         <section className="py-16 bg-gray-50 dark:bg-gray-800">
160:           <div className="container mx-auto px-4">
161:             <h2 className="text-3xl font-bold mb-8 text-center text-gray-900 dark:text-white">Share Your Success Story</h2>
162:             <div className="max-w-3xl mx-auto text-center">
163:               <p className="text-lg mb-8 text-gray-700 dark:text-gray-300">
164:                 Are you a restaurant owner who has successfully reduced dependence on Wolt? We'd love to feature your 
165:                 story on this page to inspire others. Please get in touch with us to share your experience.
166:               </p>
167:               <Link 
168:                 href="/contact"
169:                 className="inline-block px-6 py-3 bg-wolt-blue hover:bg-wolt-dark text-white rounded-lg font-medium transition duration-200"
170:               >
171:                 Contact Us to Share Your Story
172:               </Link>
173:             </div>
174:           </div>
175:         </section>
176:         <section className="py-16 bg-wolt-blue text-white">
177:           <div className="container mx-auto px-4 text-center">
178:             <h2 className="text-3xl md:text-4xl font-bold mb-8">Ready to Make the Change?</h2>
179:             <p className="text-xl mb-8 max-w-3xl mx-auto">
180:               Joining these success stories is easier than you think. Find resources and alternatives to help your 
181:               restaurant thrive without exploitative delivery fees.
182:             </p>
183:             <div className="flex flex-wrap justify-center gap-4">
184:               <Link 
185:                 href="/alternatives"
186:                 className="inline-block px-6 py-3 bg-white text-wolt-blue hover:bg-gray-100 rounded-lg font-medium transition duration-200"
187:               >
188:                 Explore Alternatives
189:               </Link>
190:               <Link 
191:                 href="/pledge"
192:                 className="inline-block px-6 py-3 bg-transparent border border-white text-white hover:bg-white/10 rounded-lg font-medium transition duration-200"
193:               >
194:                 Sign the Pledge
195:               </Link>
196:             </div>
197:           </div>
198:         </section>
199:       </main>
200:     </>
201:   );
202: }

================
File: src/app/workers/page.tsx
================
  1: 'use client';
  2: import PageHero from '@/components/PageHero';
  3: import ContentSection from '@/components/ContentSection';
  4: import Citation from '@/components/Citation';
  5: import ComparisonTable from '@/components/ComparisonTable';
  6: import Link from 'next/link';
  7: export default function WorkersPage() {
  8:   // Worker conditions comparison data
  9:   const workerConditionsData = [
 10:     {
 11:       id: 'employment-status',
 12:       label: 'Employment Status',
 13:       wolt: 'Independent "Partner"',
 14:       justEat: 'Employee (often part-time)',
 15:       direct: 'Employee',
 16:       woltHighlight: true,
 17:       justEatHighlight: false,
 18:       directHighlight: false
 19:     },
 20:     {
 21:       id: 'union-agreement',
 22:       label: 'Union Agreement',
 23:       wolt: 'No',
 24:       justEat: 'Yes (3F)',
 25:       direct: 'Varies by restaurant',
 26:       woltHighlight: true,
 27:       justEatHighlight: false,
 28:       directHighlight: false
 29:     },
 30:     {
 31:       id: 'sick-pay',
 32:       label: 'Sick Pay',
 33:       wolt: 'No',
 34:       justEat: 'Yes',
 35:       direct: 'Yes',
 36:       woltHighlight: true,
 37:       justEatHighlight: false,
 38:       directHighlight: false
 39:     },
 40:     {
 41:       id: 'holiday-pay',
 42:       label: 'Holiday Pay',
 43:       wolt: 'No',
 44:       justEat: 'Yes',
 45:       direct: 'Yes',
 46:       woltHighlight: true,
 47:       justEatHighlight: false,
 48:       directHighlight: false
 49:     },
 50:     {
 51:       id: 'pension',
 52:       label: 'Pension Contributions',
 53:       wolt: 'No',
 54:       justEat: 'Yes',
 55:       direct: 'Yes',
 56:       woltHighlight: true,
 57:       justEatHighlight: false,
 58:       directHighlight: false
 59:     },
 60:     {
 61:       id: 'insurance',
 62:       label: 'Work Injury Insurance',
 63:       wolt: 'No (limited coverage)',
 64:       justEat: 'Yes',
 65:       direct: 'Yes',
 66:       woltHighlight: true,
 67:       justEatHighlight: false,
 68:       directHighlight: false
 69:     },
 70:     {
 71:       id: 'equipment',
 72:       label: 'Equipment Costs',
 73:       wolt: 'Rider pays all',
 74:       justEat: 'Company provides',
 75:       direct: 'Company provides',
 76:       woltHighlight: true,
 77:       justEatHighlight: false,
 78:       directHighlight: false
 79:     },
 80:     {
 81:       id: 'guaranteed-hours',
 82:       label: 'Guaranteed Hours',
 83:       wolt: 'No',
 84:       justEat: 'Yes',
 85:       direct: 'Yes',
 86:       woltHighlight: true,
 87:       justEatHighlight: false,
 88:       directHighlight: false
 89:     }
 90:   ];
 91:   return (
 92:     <>
 93:       <PageHero 
 94:         title="Exploitation of Delivery Workers"
 95:         subtitle="How Wolt's business model undermines Danish labor standards"
 96:         color="danger"
 97:       />
 98:       <main>
 99:         <ContentSection 
100:           title="'Partners' Without Protections"
101:           bgColor="white"
102:         >
103:           <p className="mb-4">
104:             Wolt classifies its couriers as independent "partners" rather than employees. This distinction is crucial because it allows Wolt to avoid providing the standard benefits and protections that Danish workers typically receive.
105:           </p>
106:           <p className="mb-4">
107:             This means couriers lack:
108:           </p>
109:           <ul className="list-disc pl-6 mb-6 space-y-2">
110:             <li>Hourly wage guarantees</li>
111:             <li>Sick pay</li>
112:             <li>Holiday pay</li>
113:             <li>Insurance coverage</li>
114:             <li>Pension contributions<Citation id="1" source="Danish Labor Standards Report, 2023" /></li>
115:           </ul>
116:           <p className="mb-4">
117:             Instead, couriers shoulder all the risks and costs themselves, including:
118:           </p>
119:           <ul className="list-disc pl-6 mb-6 space-y-2">
120:             <li>Bicycle/vehicle purchase and maintenance</li>
121:             <li>Smartphone and data plans</li>
122:             <li>Accident liability</li>
123:             <li>Taxes and accounting<Citation id="2" source="Courier Costs Analysis, Copenhagen, 2023" /></li>
124:           </ul>
125:           <p>
126:             Meanwhile, Wolt avoids the employer responsibilities that are fundamental to the Danish labor model.<Citation id="3" source="Danish Labor Union Report on Gig Economy, 2023" />
127:           </p>
128:         </ContentSection>
129:         <ContentSection 
130:           title="Comparing Worker Conditions"
131:           bgColor="light"
132:         >
133:           <p className="mb-6">
134:             The table below shows how drastically different worker protections are across different delivery models:
135:           </p>
136:           <ComparisonTable 
137:             title="Worker Conditions and Benefits"
138:             items={workerConditionsData}
139:             colorScheme="workers"
140:           />
141:           <p className="mt-6">
142:             As the comparison shows, Wolt couriers lack virtually every standard employment protection that Danish workers have come to expect. In contrast, Just Eat has taken steps to provide proper employment status to its couriers, including signing a collective agreement with the 3F union in 2021.<Citation id="4" source="Just Eat-3F Collective Agreement, 2021" />
143:           </p>
144:         </ContentSection>
145:         <ContentSection 
146:           title="Low & Unstable Earnings"
147:           bgColor="white"
148:         >
149:           <p className="mb-4">
150:             Wolt riders are paid per delivery, typically about 45 DKK per drop (before tax). While Wolt advertises the potential to earn "up to" 120 DKK/hour, this is only possible during peak hours if a courier manages to secure one of the limited "shifts" and complete deliveries at maximum efficiency.<Citation id="5" source="Wolt Courier Earnings Survey, 2023" />
151:           </p>
152:           <p className="mb-4">
153:             The reality for most couriers includes:
154:           </p>
155:           <ul className="list-disc pl-6 mb-6 space-y-2">
156:             <li>No guaranteed income (effectively a zero-hour contract)</li>
157:             <li>Long waits between deliveries during off-peak hours</li>
158:             <li>Competition between couriers for available shifts</li>
159:             <li>Payment rates that have reportedly decreased over time, especially after Foodora's exit reduced competition<Citation id="6" source="Courier Interviews, Copenhagen Post, 2024" /></li>
160:           </ul>
161:           <div className="bg-red-50 dark:bg-red-900 p-4 rounded-lg border-l-4 border-red-500 my-6">
162:             <h3 className="font-semibold text-red-900 dark:text-red-100 mb-2">Real-World Impact</h3>
163:             <p className="text-red-800 dark:text-red-200">
164:               "Some weeks I work 50 hours and barely make minimum wage after expenses. When it rains or snows, we take all the risk but Wolt takes all the profit." — Anonymous Wolt courier, Copenhagen
165:             </p>
166:           </div>
167:         </ContentSection>
168:         <ContentSection 
169:           title="Pressure and Safety Risks"
170:           bgColor="light"
171:         >
172:           <p className="mb-4">
173:             Wolt's algorithm rewards speed, which increases pressure on couriers to deliver as quickly as possible. This creates serious safety risks, especially in busy urban areas like Copenhagen where couriers navigate through traffic while checking their phones for directions.<Citation id="7" source="Traffic Safety Study on Food Delivery Couriers, 2023" />
174:           </p>
175:           <p className="mb-4">
176:             The system creates multiple hazards:
177:           </p>
178:           <ul className="list-disc pl-6 mb-6 space-y-2">
179:             <li>Incentives to rush through traffic to maximize deliveries per hour</li>
180:             <li>Pressure to ride in dangerous weather conditions</li>
181:             <li>Distracted riding while managing the app interface</li>
182:             <li>Long hours leading to fatigue and decreased awareness</li>
183:           </ul>
184:           <p className="mb-4">
185:             If couriers fail to meet Wolt's strict standards (even trivial ones like how they carry a pizza), Wolt can deactivate ("fire") riders at will with little recourse.<Citation id="8" source="Courier Deactivation Policies Analysis, 2023" /> Yet, when couriers get hurt on the job, Wolt has historically claimed no responsibility as they're not "employees."
186:           </p>
187:         </ContentSection>
188:         <ContentSection 
189:           title="Authorities Push Back"
190:           bgColor="white"
191:         >
192:           <p className="mb-4">
193:             Danish authorities have started to challenge Wolt's labor model:
194:           </p>
195:           <ul className="list-disc pl-6 mb-6 space-y-3">
196:             <li>
197:               <strong>Danish Occupational Injury Authority</strong> ruled that Wolt must compensate injured couriers, effectively recognizing them as employees under the law.<Citation id="9" source="Danish Occupational Injury Authority Ruling, 2023" />
198:             </li>
199:             <li>
200:               <strong>Denmark's Immigration Service (SIRI)</strong> now treats Wolt riders as having an employer, not as true freelancers, for residency permit purposes.<Citation id="10" source="SIRI Immigration Guidelines Update, 2023" />
201:             </li>
202:             <li>
203:               <strong>3F Union</strong> has filed multiple cases challenging the contractor classification of food delivery workers.
204:             </li>
205:           </ul>
206:           <p className="mb-4">
207:             Despite these rulings, Wolt continues to fight against reclassifying couriers as employees,<Citation id="11" source="Wolt Legal Challenges Report, 2023" /> resisting the basic rights and protections that Danish workers typically enjoy.
208:           </p>
209:           <div className="bg-blue-50 dark:bg-blue-900 p-4 rounded-lg mb-6">
210:             <h3 className="font-semibold mb-2">Competitors Treat Workers Better</h3>
211:             <p className="mb-2">Unlike Wolt, Just Eat employs many of its couriers on formal contracts (often part-time) and signed a collective bargaining agreement with the 3F union in 2021.<Citation id="12" source="Just Eat-3F Collective Agreement, 2021" /></p>
212:             <p>Foodora in other Nordic countries has historically hired couriers as employees and reached union agreements (e.g., Norway in 2019 after couriers went on strike).<Citation id="13" source="Foodora Norway Union Agreement, 2019" /></p>
213:           </div>
214:         </ContentSection>
215:         <section className="py-16 bg-wolt-blue text-white">
216:           <div className="container mx-auto px-4 text-center">
217:             <h2 className="text-3xl md:text-4xl font-bold mb-8">Stand With Workers</h2>
218:             <p className="text-xl mb-8 max-w-3xl mx-auto">
219:               By choosing alternatives to Wolt, you're supporting better treatment of the people who deliver your food. Every order matters.
220:             </p>
221:             <div className="flex flex-wrap justify-center gap-4">
222:               <Link 
223:                 href="/pledge"
224:                 className="inline-block px-6 py-3 bg-white text-wolt-blue hover:bg-gray-100 rounded-lg font-medium transition duration-200"
225:               >
226:                 Sign the Pledge
227:               </Link>
228:               <Link 
229:                 href="/alternatives"
230:                 className="inline-block px-6 py-3 bg-transparent border border-white text-white hover:bg-white/10 rounded-lg font-medium transition duration-200"
231:               >
232:                 Find Alternatives
233:               </Link>
234:             </div>
235:           </div>
236:         </section>
237:       </main>
238:     </>
239:   );
240: }

================
File: src/app/globals.css
================
 1: @tailwind base;
 2: @tailwind components;
 3: @tailwind utilities;
 4: /* Import the Wolt design system */
 5: @import '../styles/wolt-design-system.css';
 6: :root {
 7:   --foreground-rgb: 0, 0, 0;
 8:   --background-start-rgb: 214, 219, 220;
 9:   --background-end-rgb: 255, 255, 255;
10: }
11: @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
12:   :root {
13:     --foreground-rgb: 255, 255, 255;
14:     --background-start-rgb: 0, 0, 0;
15:     --background-end-rgb: 0, 0, 0;
16:   }
17: }
18: body {
19:   color: var(--wolt-gray-900);
20:   background: var(--wolt-white);
21: }
22: /* Dark mode */
23: .dark body {
24:   color: var(--wolt-white);
25:   background: var(--wolt-navy);
26: }

================
File: src/components/CircleIcon.tsx
================
 1: 'use client';
 2: import React from 'react';
 3: import { motion } from 'framer-motion';
 4: interface CircleIconProps {
 5:   size?: 'small' | 'medium' | 'large';
 6:   icon: React.ReactNode;
 7:   className?: string;
 8:   animate?: boolean;
 9: }
10: export default function CircleIcon({
11:   size = 'medium',
12:   icon,
13:   className = '',
14:   animate = true
15: }: CircleIconProps) {
16:   // Size mapping
17:   const sizeMap = {
18:     small: 'w-12 h-12',
19:     medium: 'w-16 h-16',
20:     large: 'w-24 h-24'
21:   };
22:   // Icon size mapping
23:   const iconSizeMap = {
24:     small: 'w-6 h-6',
25:     medium: 'w-8 h-8',
26:     large: 'w-12 h-12'
27:   };
28:   // Base styles for circle
29:   const circleStyles = `${sizeMap[size]} rounded-full bg-wolt-blue flex items-center justify-center text-white ${className}`;
30:   // Animation variants
31:   const variants = {
32:     initial: { scale: 0.8, opacity: 0 },
33:     animate: { scale: 1, opacity: 1, transition: { duration: 0.5 } },
34:     hover: { scale: 1.05, transition: { duration: 0.2 } }
35:   };
36:   return animate ? (
37:     <motion.div 
38:       className={circleStyles}
39:       initial="initial"
40:       whileInView="animate"
41:       whileHover="hover"
42:       viewport={{ once: true }}
43:       variants={variants}
44:     >
45:       <div className={iconSizeMap[size]}>
46:         {icon}
47:       </div>
48:     </motion.div>
49:   ) : (
50:     <div className={circleStyles}>
51:       <div className={iconSizeMap[size]}>
52:         {icon}
53:       </div>
54:     </div>
55:   );
56: }

================
File: src/components/Citation.tsx
================
 1: 'use client';
 2: import { useState } from 'react';
 3: import { motion, AnimatePresence } from 'framer-motion';
 4: interface CitationProps {
 5:   id: string;
 6:   source: string;
 7:   url?: string;
 8:   description?: string;
 9: }
10: export default function Citation({ id, source, url, description }: CitationProps) {
11:   const [isOpen, setIsOpen] = useState(false);
12:   return (
13:     <span className="relative inline-block">
14:       <sup 
15:         className="text-wolt-blue dark:text-wolt-light cursor-pointer hover:underline" 
16:         onClick={() => setIsOpen(!isOpen)}
17:         aria-label={`Citation ${id}: ${source}`}
18:       >
19:         [{id}]
20:       </sup>
21:       <AnimatePresence>
22:         {isOpen && (
23:           <motion.div 
24:             initial={{ opacity: 0, y: -10 }}
25:             animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
26:             exit={{ opacity: 0, y: -10 }}
27:             transition={{ duration: 0.2 }}
28:             className="absolute bottom-full left-0 mb-2 p-3 bg-white dark:bg-gray-800 border border-gray-200 dark:border-gray-700 rounded-md shadow-lg z-10"
29:             style={{ minWidth: '250px', maxWidth: '300px' }}
30:           >
31:             <div className="text-sm text-gray-800 dark:text-gray-200">
32:               <p className="font-semibold mb-1">Source {id}:</p>
33:               {url ? (
34:                 <a 
35:                   href={url} 
36:                   target="_blank" 
37:                   rel="noopener noreferrer" 
38:                   className="text-wolt-blue dark:text-wolt-light hover:underline"
39:                 >
40:                   {source}
41:                 </a>
42:               ) : (
43:                 <p>{source}</p>
44:               )}
45:               {description && (
46:                 <p className="mt-2 text-xs text-gray-600 dark:text-gray-400">
47:                   {description}
48:                 </p>
49:               )}
50:               <button 
51:                 onClick={() => setIsOpen(false)}
52:                 className="mt-2 text-xs text-gray-500 hover:text-gray-700 dark:text-gray-400 dark:hover:text-gray-200"
53:               >
54:                 Close
55:               </button>
56:             </div>
57:           </motion.div>
58:         )}
59:       </AnimatePresence>
60:     </span>
61:   );
62: }

================
File: src/components/ComparisonTable.tsx
================
  1: 'use client';
  2: import { motion } from 'framer-motion';
  3: import { useLanguage } from '@/contexts/LanguageContext';
  4: interface ComparisonItem {
  5:   id: string;
  6:   label: string;
  7:   wolt: string | number;
  8:   justEat: string | number;
  9:   direct: string | number;
 10:   woltHighlight?: boolean;
 11:   justEatHighlight?: boolean;
 12:   directHighlight?: boolean;
 13:   unit?: string;
 14: }
 15: interface ComparisonTableProps {
 16:   title: string;
 17:   description?: string;
 18:   items: ComparisonItem[];
 19:   colorScheme?: 'default' | 'fees' | 'workers';
 20: }
 21: export default function ComparisonTable({ 
 22:   title, 
 23:   description, 
 24:   items,
 25:   colorScheme = 'default' 
 26: }: ComparisonTableProps) {
 27:   const { language } = useLanguage();
 28:   // Determine color scheme for highlighting
 29:   const getHighlightColor = (scheme: string, isGood: boolean) => {
 30:     if (scheme === 'fees') {
 31:       return isGood ? 'bg-green-100 dark:bg-green-900 text-green-800 dark:text-green-200' : 'bg-red-100 dark:bg-red-900 text-red-800 dark:text-red-200';
 32:     } else if (scheme === 'workers') {
 33:       return isGood ? 'bg-blue-100 dark:bg-blue-900 text-blue-800 dark:text-blue-200' : 'bg-yellow-100 dark:bg-yellow-900 text-yellow-800 dark:text-yellow-200';
 34:     }
 35:     return isGood ? 'bg-gray-100 dark:bg-gray-700' : 'bg-gray-200 dark:bg-gray-600';
 36:   };
 37:   return (
 38:     <div className="w-full mx-auto my-8">
 39:       <motion.div
 40:         initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 20 }}
 41:         whileInView={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
 42:         viewport={{ once: true }}
 43:         transition={{ duration: 0.5 }}
 44:       >
 45:         {title && (
 46:           <h3 className="text-xl md:text-2xl font-bold mb-4 text-center text-gray-900 dark:text-white">
 47:             {title}
 48:           </h3>
 49:         )}
 50:         {description && (
 51:           <p className="text-center mb-6 text-gray-700 dark:text-gray-300 max-w-3xl mx-auto">
 52:             {description}
 53:           </p>
 54:         )}
 55:         <div className="overflow-x-auto rounded-lg shadow">
 56:           <table className="w-full border-collapse">
 57:             <thead>
 58:               <tr className="bg-gray-100 dark:bg-gray-800">
 59:                 <th className="px-4 py-3 text-left font-semibold text-gray-700 dark:text-gray-300 border-b">
 60:                   {language === 'en' ? 'Feature' : 'Funktion'}
 61:                 </th>
 62:                 <th className="px-4 py-3 text-center font-semibold text-wolt-blue dark:text-wolt-light border-b">
 63:                   Wolt
 64:                 </th>
 65:                 <th className="px-4 py-3 text-center font-semibold text-gray-700 dark:text-gray-300 border-b">
 66:                   Just Eat
 67:                 </th>
 68:                 <th className="px-4 py-3 text-center font-semibold text-gray-700 dark:text-gray-300 border-b">
 69:                   {language === 'en' ? 'Direct Order' : 'Direkte Bestilling'}
 70:                 </th>
 71:               </tr>
 72:             </thead>
 73:             <tbody>
 74:               {items.map((item, index) => (
 75:                 <tr 
 76:                   key={item.id} 
 77:                   className={index % 2 === 0 ? 'bg-white dark:bg-gray-900' : 'bg-gray-50 dark:bg-gray-800'}
 78:                 >
 79:                   <td className="px-4 py-3 text-left border-b text-gray-800 dark:text-gray-200 font-medium">
 80:                     {item.label}
 81:                   </td>
 82:                   <td className={`px-4 py-3 text-center border-b ${item.woltHighlight === false ? getHighlightColor(colorScheme, true) : item.woltHighlight === true ? getHighlightColor(colorScheme, false) : ''}`}>
 83:                     {typeof item.wolt === 'number' && item.unit 
 84:                       ? `${item.wolt}${item.unit}`
 85:                       : item.wolt}
 86:                   </td>
 87:                   <td className={`px-4 py-3 text-center border-b ${item.justEatHighlight === true ? getHighlightColor(colorScheme, true) : item.justEatHighlight === false ? getHighlightColor(colorScheme, false) : ''}`}>
 88:                     {typeof item.justEat === 'number' && item.unit 
 89:                       ? `${item.justEat}${item.unit}`
 90:                       : item.justEat}
 91:                   </td>
 92:                   <td className={`px-4 py-3 text-center border-b ${item.directHighlight === true ? getHighlightColor(colorScheme, true) : item.directHighlight === false ? getHighlightColor(colorScheme, false) : ''}`}>
 93:                     {typeof item.direct === 'number' && item.unit 
 94:                       ? `${item.direct}${item.unit}`
 95:                       : item.direct}
 96:                   </td>
 97:                 </tr>
 98:               ))}
 99:             </tbody>
100:           </table>
101:         </div>
102:       </motion.div>
103:     </div>
104:   );
105: }

================
File: src/components/ContentSection.tsx
================
 1: 'use client';
 2: import { ReactNode } from 'react';
 3: import { motion } from 'framer-motion';
 4: import ImageContainer from './ImageContainer';
 5: interface ContentSectionProps {
 6:   title?: string;
 7:   children: ReactNode;
 8:   imageUrl?: string;
 9:   imageAlt?: string;
10:   imagePosition?: 'left' | 'right';
11:   bgColor?: 'light' | 'dark';
12:   id?: string;
13:   className?: string;
14: }
15: export default function ContentSection({
16:   title,
17:   children,
18:   imageUrl,
19:   imageAlt = '',
20:   imagePosition = 'right',
21:   bgColor = 'light',
22:   id,
23:   className = '',
24: }: ContentSectionProps) {
25:   const bgColorClass = 
26:     bgColor === 'light' ? 'wolt-content-section-light' : 'wolt-content-section-dark';
27:   return (
28:     <section id={id} className={`wolt-content-section ${bgColorClass} ${className}`}>
29:       <div className="container mx-auto px-4">
30:         {title && (
31:           <motion.h2 
32:             initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 20 }}
33:             whileInView={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
34:             viewport={{ once: true }}
35:             transition={{ duration: 0.5 }}
36:             className="text-3xl md:text-4xl font-bold mb-8 text-center text-[--wolt-gray-900] dark:text-white"
37:           >
38:             {title}
39:           </motion.h2>
40:         )}
41:         <div className={`flex flex-col ${imagePosition === 'left' ? 'md:flex-row-reverse' : 'md:flex-row'} gap-8 items-start`}>
42:           <motion.div 
43:             initial={{ opacity: 0, x: imagePosition === 'left' ? 20 : -20 }}
44:             whileInView={{ opacity: 1, x: 0 }}
45:             viewport={{ once: true }}
46:             transition={{ duration: 0.5, delay: 0.2 }}
47:             className={`w-full ${imageUrl ? 'md:w-7/12' : 'md:w-full'}`}
48:           >
49:             <div className="prose prose-lg max-w-none dark:prose-invert">
50:               {children}
51:             </div>
52:           </motion.div>
53:           {imageUrl && (
54:             <motion.div 
55:               initial={{ opacity: 0, scale: 0.9 }}
56:               whileInView={{ opacity: 1, scale: 1 }}
57:               viewport={{ once: true }}
58:               transition={{ duration: 0.5, delay: 0.3 }}
59:               className="w-full md:w-5/12"
60:             >
61:               {imageUrl.startsWith('/') ? (
62:                 <ImageContainer
63:                   src={imageUrl}
64:                   alt={imageAlt}
65:                   height={320}
66:                   className="rounded-lg shadow-md"
67:                   priority
68:                 />
69:               ) : (
70:                 <img 
71:                   src={imageUrl} 
72:                   alt={imageAlt}
73:                   className="w-full h-auto rounded-lg shadow-md" 
74:                 />
75:               )}
76:             </motion.div>
77:           )}
78:         </div>
79:       </div>
80:     </section>
81:   );
82: }

================
File: src/components/LanguageSwitcher.tsx
================
 1: 'use client';
 2: import { useState, useEffect } from 'react';
 3: import { useLanguage } from '@/contexts/LanguageContext';
 4: import { motion } from 'framer-motion';
 5: export default function LanguageSwitcher() {
 6:   const { language, setLanguage } = useLanguage();
 7:   const [isOpen, setIsOpen] = useState(false);
 8:   // Close dropdown when clicking outside
 9:   useEffect(() => {
10:     const handleClickOutside = (event: MouseEvent) => {
11:       const target = event.target as HTMLElement;
12:       if (!target.closest('.language-switcher-container')) {
13:         setIsOpen(false);
14:       }
15:     };
16:     document.addEventListener('mousedown', handleClickOutside);
17:     return () => {
18:       document.removeEventListener('mousedown', handleClickOutside);
19:     };
20:   }, []);
21:   const toggleLanguage = (lang: 'en' | 'da') => {
22:     setLanguage(lang);
23:     setIsOpen(false);
24:   };
25:   return (
26:     <div className="relative language-switcher-container">
27:       <button
28:         aria-label="Change language"
29:         className="flex items-center text-gray-800 dark:text-gray-200 hover:text-wolt-blue dark:hover:text-wolt-light transition-colors"
30:         onClick={() => setIsOpen(!isOpen)}
31:       >
32:         <span className="mr-1 font-medium">{language === 'en' ? '🇬🇧 EN' : '🇩🇰 DA'}</span>
33:         <svg 
34:           xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" 
35:           className={`h-4 w-4 transition-transform ${isOpen ? 'transform rotate-180' : ''}`} 
36:           fill="none" 
37:           viewBox="0 0 24 24" 
38:           stroke="currentColor"
39:         >
40:           <path strokeLinecap="round" strokeLinejoin="round" strokeWidth={2} d="M19 9l-7 7-7-7" />
41:         </svg>
42:       </button>
43:       {isOpen && (
44:         <motion.div
45:           initial={{ opacity: 0, y: -10 }}
46:           animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
47:           exit={{ opacity: 0, y: -10 }}
48:           transition={{ duration: 0.2 }}
49:           className="absolute top-full right-0 mt-2 bg-white dark:bg-gray-800 rounded-md shadow-lg overflow-hidden z-20 w-32"
50:         >
51:           <div className="py-1">
52:             <button
53:               onClick={() => toggleLanguage('en')}
54:               className={`block w-full text-left px-4 py-2 text-sm ${
55:                 language === 'en' 
56:                   ? 'bg-gray-100 dark:bg-gray-700 text-wolt-blue dark:text-wolt-light font-medium' 
57:                   : 'text-gray-800 dark:text-gray-200 hover:bg-gray-50 dark:hover:bg-gray-700'
58:               }`}
59:             >
60:               🇬🇧 English
61:             </button>
62:             <button
63:               onClick={() => toggleLanguage('da')}
64:               className={`block w-full text-left px-4 py-2 text-sm ${
65:                 language === 'da' 
66:                   ? 'bg-gray-100 dark:bg-gray-700 text-wolt-blue dark:text-wolt-light font-medium' 
67:                   : 'text-gray-800 dark:text-gray-200 hover:bg-gray-50 dark:hover:bg-gray-700'
68:               }`}
69:             >
70:               🇩🇰 Dansk
71:             </button>
72:           </div>
73:         </motion.div>
74:       )}
75:     </div>
76:   );
77: }

================
File: src/components/OmnesFontLoader.tsx
================
 1: 'use client';
 2: import React, { useEffect } from 'react';
 3: export default function OmnesFontLoader() {
 4:   useEffect(() => {
 5:     // Create a style element to load the fonts
 6:     const style = document.createElement('style');
 7:     // Define the font faces for Omnes family
 8:     style.textContent = `
 9:       @font-face {
10:         font-family: 'Omnes';
11:         src: url('/Omnes Font Family/Omnes Regular.ttf') format('truetype');
12:         font-weight: 400;
13:         font-style: normal;
14:         font-display: swap;
15:       }
16:       @font-face {
17:         font-family: 'Omnes';
18:         src: url('/Omnes Font Family/Omnes Bold.ttf') format('truetype');
19:         font-weight: 700;
20:         font-style: normal;
21:         font-display: swap;
22:       }
23:       @font-face {
24:         font-family: 'Omnes';
25:         src: url('/Omnes Font Family/Omnes SemiBold.ttf') format('truetype');
26:         font-weight: 600;
27:         font-style: normal;
28:         font-display: swap;
29:       }
30:       @font-face {
31:         font-family: 'Omnes';
32:         src: url('/Omnes Font Family/Omnes Medium.ttf') format('truetype');
33:         font-weight: 500;
34:         font-style: normal;
35:         font-display: swap;
36:       }
37:       @font-face {
38:         font-family: 'Omnes';
39:         src: url('/Omnes Font Family/Omnes Light.ttf') format('truetype');
40:         font-weight: 300;
41:         font-style: normal;
42:         font-display: swap;
43:       }
44:     `;
45:     // Add the style element to the head
46:     document.head.appendChild(style);
47:     // Clean up the style element on component unmount
48:     return () => {
49:       document.head.removeChild(style);
50:     };
51:   }, []);
52:   // This component doesn't render anything visible
53:   return null;
54: }

================
File: src/components/PageHero.tsx
================
 1: 'use client';
 2: import { motion } from 'framer-motion';
 3: import ImageContainer from './ImageContainer';
 4: interface PageHeroProps {
 5:   title: string;
 6:   subtitle?: string;
 7:   imageUrl?: string;
 8:   color?: 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'danger';
 9: }
10: export default function PageHero({ title, subtitle, imageUrl, color = 'primary' }: PageHeroProps) {
11:   // Determine background color based on the color prop
12:   const bgColorClass = 
13:     color === 'primary' ? 'bg-[--wolt-cyan]' : 
14:     color === 'secondary' ? 'bg-[--wolt-navy]' : 
15:     'bg-[--wolt-error]';
16:   return (
17:     <section className={`wolt-hero ${bgColorClass}`}>
18:       <div className="container mx-auto px-4">
19:         <div className="flex flex-col md:flex-row items-center md:items-start justify-between">
20:           <div className="w-full md:w-1/2 mb-8 md:mb-0">
21:             <motion.h1 
22:               initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 20 }}
23:               animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
24:               transition={{ duration: 0.5 }}
25:               className="text-4xl md:text-5xl lg:text-6xl font-bold text-white mb-4"
26:             >
27:               {title}
28:             </motion.h1>
29:             {subtitle && (
30:               <motion.p 
31:                 initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 20 }}
32:                 animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
33:                 transition={{ duration: 0.5, delay: 0.1 }}
34:                 className="text-xl md:text-2xl text-white/90"
35:               >
36:                 {subtitle}
37:               </motion.p>
38:             )}
39:           </div>
40:           {imageUrl && (
41:             <motion.div 
42:               initial={{ opacity: 0, x: 20 }}
43:               animate={{ opacity: 1, x: 0 }}
44:               transition={{ duration: 0.5, delay: 0.2 }}
45:               className="w-full md:w-1/2 flex justify-center"
46:             >
47:               <div className="w-full max-w-md">
48:                 <ImageContainer
49:                   src={imageUrl}
50:                   alt={title}
51:                   height={300}
52:                   priority
53:                 />
54:               </div>
55:             </motion.div>
56:           )}
57:         </div>
58:       </div>
59:     </section>
60:   );
61: }

================
File: src/components/SkipToContent.tsx
================
 1: export default function SkipToContent() {
 2:   return (
 3:     <a
 4:       href="#main-content"
 5:       className="sr-only focus:not-sr-only focus:absolute focus:z-50 focus:p-4 focus:bg-white focus:text-wolt-blue focus:border-2 focus:border-wolt-blue"
 6:     >
 7:       Skip to content
 8:     </a>
 9:   );
10: }

================
File: src/components/StatDisplay.tsx
================
 1: 'use client';
 2: import React from 'react';
 3: import { motion } from 'framer-motion';
 4: import CircleIcon from './CircleIcon';
 5: import { useInView } from 'framer-motion';
 6: interface StatDisplayProps {
 7:   value: string | number;
 8:   label: string;
 9:   icon?: React.ReactNode;
10:   animate?: boolean;
11:   className?: string;
12: }
13: export default function StatDisplay({
14:   value,
15:   label,
16:   icon,
17:   animate = true,
18:   className = ''
19: }: StatDisplayProps) {
20:   const ref = React.useRef(null);
21:   const isInView = useInView(ref, { once: true });
22:   // Animation variants
23:   const textVariants = {
24:     hidden: { opacity: 0, y: 20 },
25:     visible: { 
26:       opacity: 1, 
27:       y: 0,
28:       transition: { 
29:         duration: 0.5,
30:         delay: 0.2
31:       }
32:     }
33:   };
34:   return (
35:     <div className={`flex flex-col items-center text-center ${className}`} ref={ref}>
36:       {icon && <CircleIcon icon={icon} animate={animate} className="mb-4" />}
37:       {animate ? (
38:         <motion.div
39:           initial="hidden"
40:           animate={isInView ? "visible" : "hidden"}
41:           variants={textVariants}
42:           className="flex flex-col items-center"
43:         >
44:           <span className="font-omnes font-bold text-4xl md:text-5xl lg:text-6xl mb-2">
45:             {value}
46:           </span>
47:           <span className="text-gray-600 dark:text-gray-300 text-lg">
48:             {label}
49:           </span>
50:         </motion.div>
51:       ) : (
52:         <div className="flex flex-col items-center">
53:           <span className="font-omnes font-bold text-4xl md:text-5xl lg:text-6xl mb-2">
54:             {value}
55:           </span>
56:           <span className="text-gray-600 dark:text-gray-300 text-lg">
57:             {label}
58:           </span>
59:         </div>
60:       )}
61:     </div>
62:   );
63: }

================
File: src/components/StyleGuide.md
================
  1: # Cancel-Wolt: Design & Style Guide
  2: 
  3: This document provides a comprehensive guide for recreating Wolt's visual identity in our protest website. The goal is to maintain visual familiarity while presenting critical information about Wolt's business practices.
  4: 
  5: ## 1. Color Palette
  6: 
  7: ### Primary Colors
  8: - **Wolt Blue:** `#009DE0` - The defining brand color, used for primary buttons, icons, and accents
  9: - **White:** `#FFFFFF` - Used for text on dark backgrounds and card backgrounds
 10: - **Black:** `#000000` - Used for dark backgrounds and some text elements
 11: 
 12: ### Secondary Colors
 13: - **Light Gray:** `#F5F5F5` - Used for alternate section backgrounds
 14: - **Dark Gray:** `#333333` - Used for body text
 15: - **Error Red:** `#FF4444` - Used to highlight negative aspects of Wolt's practices
 16: - **Success Green:** `#44CC88` - Used to highlight positive alternatives
 17: 
 18: ## 2. Typography
 19: 
 20: ### Font Families
 21: - **Headings:** Omnes Bold - Used for all headings and large display text
 22: - **Body:** Inter - Used for body text and UI elements
 23: 
 24: ### Font Sizes
 25: - **Hero Headline:** 48px (mobile: 36px)
 26: - **Section Headings:** 36px (mobile: 28px)
 27: - **Subheadings:** 24px (mobile: 20px)
 28: - **Body Text:** 16px (mobile: 16px)
 29: - **Small Text/Captions:** 14px (mobile: 14px)
 30: 
 31: ### Font Weights
 32: - **Headings:** Bold (700)
 33: - **Subheadings:** SemiBold (600)
 34: - **Body:** Regular (400)
 35: - **Emphasized text:** Medium (500)
 36: 
 37: ## 3. Components
 38: 
 39: ### Buttons
 40: - **Primary Button:** Wolt Blue background (#009DE0), white text, fully rounded corners (8px radius), padding 12px 24px
 41: - **Secondary Button:** White background, Wolt Blue text and border, same rounding and padding
 42: - **Hover states:** Slight darkening (10%) of the background color, with a subtle scale transform (1.02)
 43: 
 44: ### Cards
 45: - White background
 46: - Light shadow: `0 4px 6px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05)`
 47: - Rounded corners (12px radius)
 48: - Padding: 24px
 49: - Optional Wolt Blue accent (top border or icon)
 50: 
 51: ### Icons
 52: - Circular background in Wolt Blue (#009DE0)
 53: - White icon within the circle
 54: - Simple, minimal line-style icons
 55: - Size: 48px diameter for feature highlighting, 24px for UI elements
 56: 
 57: ### Statistics Display
 58: - Large numbers in Omnes Bold (48px+)
 59: - Smaller label text underneath
 60: - Often displayed in groups of 3-4 across the page
 61: - Each statistic accompanied by a circular icon
 62: 
 63: ## 4. Layout & Spacing
 64: 
 65: ### Grid System
 66: - 12-column grid for desktop
 67: - 4-column grid for mobile
 68: - Gutter: 24px (desktop), 16px (mobile)
 69: 
 70: ### Section Spacing
 71: - Vertical spacing between sections: 80px (desktop), 60px (mobile)
 72: - Section padding: 80px 0 (desktop), 60px 0 (mobile)
 73: 
 74: ### Content Width
 75: - Maximum content width: 1200px
 76: - Edge padding: 24px (desktop), 16px (mobile)
 77: 
 78: ## 5. UI Patterns
 79: 
 80: ### Hero Sections
 81: - Dark background (black or very dark blue)
 82: - Large headline in Omnes Bold
 83: - Short, impactful subtitle
 84: - Primary CTA button
 85: - Full-width design with centered text
 86: 
 87: ### Content Sections
 88: - Alternating background colors (white and light gray)
 89: - Consistent width content container
 90: - Section heading at the top, centered
 91: - Content follows in a clean, readable layout
 92: 
 93: ### Navigation
 94: - Simple horizontal navigation
 95: - Wolt Blue highlight for active page
 96: - Mobile: Hamburger menu with slide-in panel
 97: 
 98: ## 6. Illustrations & Imagery
 99: 
100: ### Style
101: - Simplified, cartoon-like illustrations
102: - Flat design with minimal shading
103: - Limited color palette with Wolt Blue as a prominent color
104: - Rounded edges and friendly appearance
105: 
106: ### Usage
107: - Hero backgrounds
108: - Section accents
109: - Feature explanations
110: - Statistical representation
111: 
112: ## 7. Animations & Interactions
113: 
114: ### Page Transitions
115: - Subtle fade-in effects when loading new pages
116: - Smooth scrolling for in-page navigation
117: 
118: ### Content Animations
119: - Elements fade and slide up as they enter the viewport
120: - Statistics counters animate from 0 to their final value
121: - Subtle hover effects on interactive elements
122: 
123: ### Button States
124: - Hover: Scale slightly (1.02) and lighten/darken
125: - Active: Scale down slightly (0.98)
126: - Focus: Visible outline for accessibility
127: 
128: ## 8. Responsive Design
129: 
130: ### Breakpoints
131: - Mobile: 0-639px
132: - Tablet: 640px-1023px
133: - Desktop: 1024px+
134: 
135: ### Mobile Adaptations
136: - Stacked layout instead of side-by-side content
137: - Simplified navigation via hamburger menu
138: - Larger touch targets for buttons (min 44px height)
139: - Reduced horizontal padding
140: - Simplified statistics display
141: 
142: ## 9. Accessibility Considerations
143: 
144: While mimicking Wolt's style, we must ensure:
145: - Color contrast meets WCAG 2.1 AA standard (4.5:1 for normal text)
146: - Interactive elements are keyboard accessible
147: - Focus states are clearly visible
148: - Text remains readable at various sizes
149: - Alt text for all images and icons
150: 
151: ## 10. Implementation Notes
152: 
153: - Use Tailwind CSS with extended theme configuration
154: - Set up font loading optimization for Omnes and Inter
155: - Create reusable React components for buttons, cards, and icons
156: - Use Framer Motion for animations
157: - Implement dark mode variations of all components

================
File: src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx
================
 1: 'use client';
 2: import React, { createContext, useContext, useState, useEffect } from 'react';
 3: type Theme = 'light' | 'dark';
 4: interface ThemeContextType {
 5:   theme: Theme;
 6:   toggleTheme: () => void;
 7: }
 8: const ThemeContext = createContext<ThemeContextType>({
 9:   theme: 'light',
10:   toggleTheme: () => {},
11: });
12: export const useTheme = () => useContext(ThemeContext);
13: export default function ThemeProvider({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
14:   const [theme, setTheme] = useState<Theme>('light');
15:   useEffect(() => {
16:     // Check for user preference
17:     const savedTheme = localStorage.getItem('theme') as Theme;
18:     const prefersDark = window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').matches;
19:     if (savedTheme) {
20:       setTheme(savedTheme);
21:       document.documentElement.classList.toggle('dark', savedTheme === 'dark');
22:     } else if (prefersDark) {
23:       setTheme('dark');
24:       document.documentElement.classList.add('dark');
25:     }
26:   }, []);
27:   const toggleTheme = () => {
28:     const newTheme = theme === 'light' ? 'dark' : 'light';
29:     setTheme(newTheme);
30:     document.documentElement.classList.toggle('dark', newTheme === 'dark');
31:     localStorage.setItem('theme', newTheme);
32:   };
33:   return (
34:     <ThemeContext.Provider value={{ theme, toggleTheme }}>
35:       {children}
36:     </ThemeContext.Provider>
37:   );
38: }

================
File: src/components/WoltButton.tsx
================
 1: 'use client';
 2: import React, { ButtonHTMLAttributes } from 'react';
 3: import { motion } from 'framer-motion';
 4: interface WoltButtonProps extends ButtonHTMLAttributes<HTMLButtonElement> {
 5:   variant?: 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary';
 6:   size?: 'small' | 'medium' | 'large';
 7:   fullWidth?: boolean;
 8:   children: React.ReactNode;
 9: }
10: export default function WoltButton({
11:   variant = 'primary',
12:   size = 'medium',
13:   fullWidth = false,
14:   children,
15:   className = '',
16:   ...props
17: }: WoltButtonProps) {
18:   // Base styles - using wolt design system
19:   const baseStyles = 'wolt-btn';
20:   // Size styles
21:   const sizeStyles = {
22:     small: 'text-sm py-2 px-3 min-h-[36px]',
23:     medium: 'text-base py-2.5 px-4 min-h-[44px]',
24:     large: 'text-lg py-3 px-6 min-h-[52px]'
25:   };
26:   // Variant styles - using wolt design system
27:   const variantStyles = {
28:     primary: 'wolt-btn-primary',
29:     secondary: 'wolt-btn-secondary',
30:     tertiary: 'bg-transparent text-[--wolt-cyan] hover:bg-[--wolt-gray-50]'
31:   };
32:   // Width styles
33:   const widthStyles = fullWidth ? 'w-full' : '';
34:   // Combine all styles
35:   const buttonStyles = `${baseStyles} ${sizeStyles[size]} ${variantStyles[variant]} ${widthStyles} ${className}`;
36:   return (
37:     <motion.button
38:       className={buttonStyles}
39:       whileHover={{ scale: 1.02 }}
40:       whileTap={{ scale: 0.98 }}
41:       {...props}
42:     >
43:       {children}
44:     </motion.button>
45:   );
46: }

================
File: src/components/WoltCard.tsx
================
 1: 'use client';
 2: import React from 'react';
 3: import { motion } from 'framer-motion';
 4: interface WoltCardProps {
 5:   children: React.ReactNode;
 6:   className?: string;
 7:   accentColor?: boolean;
 8:   onClick?: () => void;
 9:   interactive?: boolean;
10: }
11: export default function WoltCard({
12:   children,
13:   className = '',
14:   accentColor = false,
15:   onClick,
16:   interactive = false
17: }: WoltCardProps) {
18:   // Base styles for the card
19:   const baseStyles = `
20:     bg-white dark:bg-gray-800 
21:     rounded-xl 
22:     shadow-sm 
23:     overflow-hidden 
24:     ${accentColor ? 'border-t-4 border-wolt-blue' : ''}
25:     ${interactive ? 'cursor-pointer transition-all duration-200' : ''}
26:     ${className}
27:   `;
28:   // Animation variants for interactive cards
29:   const variants = {
30:     initial: { scale: 1 },
31:     hover: { scale: 1.02, y: -4, boxShadow: '0 10px 15px -3px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1), 0 4px 6px -2px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05)' },
32:     tap: { scale: 0.98 }
33:   };
34:   return interactive ? (
35:     <motion.div 
36:       className={baseStyles}
37:       onClick={onClick}
38:       initial="initial"
39:       whileHover="hover"
40:       whileTap="tap"
41:       variants={variants}
42:     >
43:       {children}
44:     </motion.div>
45:   ) : (
46:     <div className={baseStyles}>
47:       {children}
48:     </div>
49:   );
50: }

================
File: src/contexts/LanguageContext.tsx
================
  1: 'use client';
  2: import { createContext, useContext, useState, useEffect, ReactNode } from 'react';
  3: type Language = 'en' | 'da';
  4: interface LanguageContextType {
  5:   language: Language;
  6:   setLanguage: (lang: Language) => void;
  7:   translations: Record<string, Record<string, Record<string, string>>>;
  8:   t: (key: string) => string;
  9: }
 10: // Create the language context with default values
 11: const LanguageContext = createContext<LanguageContextType>({
 12:   language: 'en',
 13:   setLanguage: () => {},
 14:   translations: {},
 15:   t: (key: string) => key,
 16: });
 17: // Common translations for all components
 18: const translations: Record<string, Record<string, Record<string, string>>> = {
 19:   common: {
 20:     nav: {
 21:       home: {
 22:         en: 'Home',
 23:         da: 'Hjem',
 24:       },
 25:       about: {
 26:         en: 'About',
 27:         da: 'Om Os',
 28:       },
 29:       pricing: {
 30:         en: 'Pricing',
 31:         da: 'Priser',
 32:       },
 33:       workers: {
 34:         en: 'Workers',
 35:         da: 'Arbejdere',
 36:       },
 37:       restaurants: {
 38:         en: 'Restaurants',
 39:         da: 'Restauranter',
 40:       },
 41:       alternatives: {
 42:         en: 'Alternatives',
 43:         da: 'Alternativer',
 44:       },
 45:       'success-stories': {
 46:         en: 'Success Stories',
 47:         da: 'Succeshistorier',
 48:       },
 49:       pledge: {
 50:         en: 'Pledge',
 51:         da: 'Løftet',
 52:       },
 53:       contact: {
 54:         en: 'Contact',
 55:         da: 'Kontakt',
 56:       },
 57:     },
 58:     button: {
 59:       'sign-pledge': {
 60:         en: 'Sign the Pledge',
 61:         da: 'Skriv under på løftet',
 62:       },
 63:       'find-alternatives': {
 64:         en: 'Find Alternatives',
 65:         da: 'Find alternativer',
 66:       },
 67:       'learn-more': {
 68:         en: 'Learn More',
 69:         da: 'Læs mere',
 70:       },
 71:       'contact-us': {
 72:         en: 'Contact Us',
 73:         da: 'Kontakt os',
 74:       },
 75:     },
 76:     footer: {
 77:       rights: {
 78:         en: 'All Rights Reserved',
 79:         da: 'Alle rettigheder forbeholdes',
 80:       },
 81:       privacy: {
 82:         en: 'Privacy Policy',
 83:         da: 'Privatlivspolitik',
 84:       },
 85:     },
 86:   },
 87: };
 88: export const LanguageProvider: React.FC<{ children: ReactNode }> = ({ children }) => {
 89:   // Try to get the saved language from localStorage, default to 'en'
 90:   const [language, setLanguageState] = useState<Language>('en');
 91:   // Load saved language preference from localStorage on component mount
 92:   useEffect(() => {
 93:     const savedLanguage = localStorage.getItem('language') as Language;
 94:     if (savedLanguage && (savedLanguage === 'en' || savedLanguage === 'da')) {
 95:       setLanguageState(savedLanguage);
 96:     }
 97:   }, []);
 98:   // Function to set language and save to localStorage
 99:   const setLanguage = (lang: Language) => {
100:     setLanguageState(lang);
101:     localStorage.setItem('language', lang);
102:   };
103:   // Translation helper function
104:   const t = (key: string): string => {
105:     const keys = key.split('.');
106:     let current: any = translations;
107:     // Navigate through the translation object
108:     for (const k of keys) {
109:       if (current[k] === undefined) {
110:         return key; // Return the key if translation not found
111:       }
112:       current = current[k];
113:     }
114:     // Return the translation in the current language, or fall back to English
115:     return current[language] || current['en'] || key;
116:   };
117:   return (
118:     <LanguageContext.Provider value={{ language, setLanguage, translations, t }}>
119:       {children}
120:     </LanguageContext.Provider>
121:   );
122: };
123: // Custom hook to use the language context
124: export const useLanguage = () => useContext(LanguageContext);

================
File: .eslintrc.js
================
 1: module.exports = {
 2:   parser: '@typescript-eslint/parser',
 3:   extends: [
 4:     'eslint:recommended',
 5:     'plugin:@typescript-eslint/recommended',
 6:   ],
 7:   plugins: ['@typescript-eslint'],
 8:   env: {
 9:     browser: true,
10:     node: true,
11:     es6: true,
12:   },
13:   rules: {
14:     '@typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars': ['error', { argsIgnorePattern: '^_' }],
15:     '@typescript-eslint/explicit-function-return-type': 'off',
16:     '@typescript-eslint/explicit-module-boundary-types': 'off',
17:     'no-console': ['warn', { allow: ['warn', 'error'] }],
18:   },
19: };
