2: use-pnpm=true
3: save-exact=true
4: auto-install-peers=true
5: strict-peer-dependencies=false

================
File: .nvmrc
================
1: 20

================
File: .prettierrc
================
1: {
2:   "semi": true,
3:   "singleQuote": true,
4:   "tabWidth": 2,
5:   "trailingComma": "es5",
6:   "printWidth": 100,
7:   "bracketSpacing": true,
8:   "arrowParens": "avoid"
9: }

================
File: commitlint.config.js
================
 1: module.exports = {
 2:   extends: ['@commitlint/config-conventional'],
 3:   rules: {
 4:     'body-max-line-length': [2, 'always', 100],
 5:     'subject-case': [
 6:       2,
 7:       'never',
 8:       ['sentence-case', 'start-case', 'pascal-case', 'upper-case'],
 9:     ],
10:   },
11: };

================
File: README.md
================
  1: # Cancel-Wolt Protest Website
  2: 
  3: A website to raise awareness about Wolt's unfair business practices, high commissions, and gig-worker model, while promoting fairer alternatives.
  4: 
  5: ## Features
  6: 
  7: - Informational pages on pricing, worker conditions, and impact on restaurants
  8: - Comparison tables showing differences between Wolt, Just Eat, and direct ordering
  9: - Language switching between English and Danish
 10: - Pledge system to gather support for the movement
 11: 
 12: ## Setup
 13: 
 14: ### Prerequisites
 15: 
 16: - Node.js (version specified in `.nvmrc`)
 17: - npm or yarn
 18: - A Supabase account (free tier works for development)
 19: 
 20: ### Installation
 21: 
 22: 1. Clone the repository
 23: 2. Install dependencies:
 24:    ```bash
 25:    npm install
 26:    ```
 27: 3. Copy the example environment file:
 28:    ```bash
 29:    cp env.local.example .env.local
 30:    ```
 31: 4. Update `.env.local` with your own values
 32: 
 33: ### Supabase Setup for Pledge System
 34: 
 35: 1. Create a Supabase project at [supabase.com](https://supabase.com)
 36: 2. Once your project is created, go to the SQL Editor
 37: 3. Create a new query and paste the following SQL to set up the pledges table:
 38: 
 39: ```sql
 40: -- Create pledges table for storing user signatures
 41: CREATE TABLE pledges (
 42:   id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
 43:   created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
 44:   name TEXT NOT NULL,
 45:   email TEXT NOT NULL,
 46:   comment TEXT,
 47:   verified BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
 48:   verification_token UUID NOT NULL,
 49:   
 50:   -- Add constraints
 51:   CONSTRAINT email_unique UNIQUE (email)
 52: );
 53: 
 54: -- Enable Row Level Security
 55: ALTER TABLE pledges ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
 56: 
 57: -- Create policy to allow inserts from anyone (for new pledges)
 58: CREATE POLICY "Allow inserts to pledges" 
 59:   ON pledges FOR INSERT 
 60:   WITH CHECK (true);
 61: 
 62: -- Create policy to allow reads of verified pledges by anyone
 63: CREATE POLICY "Allow anyone to read verified pledges" 
 64:   ON pledges FOR SELECT 
 65:   USING (verified = true);
 66: 
 67: -- Create policy to allow updates only with verification token
 68: CREATE POLICY "Allow updates with verification token" 
 69:   ON pledges FOR UPDATE 
 70:   USING (true)
 71:   WITH CHECK (verification_token::text = current_setting('request.jwt.claims', true)::json->>'verification_token');
 72: 
 73: -- Create function to get total verified pledge count
 74: CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION get_verified_pledge_count()
 75: RETURNS INT 
 76: LANGUAGE SQL SECURITY DEFINER
 77: AS $$
 78:   SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pledges WHERE verified = true;
 79: $$;
 80: ```
 81: 
 82: 4. Find your Supabase URL and anon key in the API settings
 83: 5. Add them to your `.env.local` file:
 84:    ```
 85:    NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=https://your-project-id.supabase.co
 86:    NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=your-anon-key
 87:    ```
 88: 
 89: ### Development
 90: 
 91: Run the development server:
 92: 
 93: ```bash
 94: npm run dev
 95: ```
 96: 
 97: Visit [http://localhost:3000](http://localhost:3000) to see the website.
 98: 
 99: ### Testing the Pledge System
100: 
101: For development, the verification process is simplified:
102: 
103: 1. Fill out and submit the pledge form
104: 2. The verification link will be:
105:    - Printed to the console
106:    - Shown on screen after submission in development mode
107:    - Auto-verified for the site owner (if using email jesper_vang@me.com)
108: 
109: For production, you should set up proper email verification.
110: 
111: ## Deployment
112: 
113: The site is ready to be deployed to Vercel:
114: 
115: 1. Push your code to a Git repository
116: 2. Connect your repository to Vercel
117: 3. Set up the environment variables in Vercel's settings
118: 4. Deploy!

================
File: tsconfig.json
================
 1: {
 2:   "compilerOptions": {
 3:     "target": "es5",
 4:     "lib": ["dom", "dom.iterable", "esnext"],
 5:     "allowJs": true,
 6:     "skipLibCheck": true,
 7:     "strict": true,
 8:     "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true,
 9:     "noEmit": true,
10:     "esModuleInterop": true,
11:     "module": "esnext",
12:     "moduleResolution": "bundler",
13:     "resolveJsonModule": true,
14:     "isolatedModules": true,
15:     "jsx": "preserve",
16:     "incremental": true,
17:     "plugins": [
18:       {
19:         "name": "next"
20:       }
21:     ],
22:     "paths": {
23:       "@/*": ["./src/*"]
24:     }
25:   },
26:   "include": ["next-env.d.ts", "**/*.ts", "**/*.tsx", ".next/types/**/*.ts"],
27:   "exclude": ["node_modules"]
28: }

================
File: wolt.md
================
 1: Start working these three scenarios sequentially, and be sure to use multiple real-world analytical frameworks for each of these scenarios.
 2: Scenario One: I am a Senior Product manager and I want to create a Business Requirements Document (BRD). Here are the high-level specs for this product: use the <SPECS GO HERE> as starting input. 
 3: 
 4: Scenario Two: I am a Senior Product manager and I want to create a Product Requirements Document (PRD). Here are the business requirements for this product: <BRD GOES HERE>
 5: 
 6: Scenario Three: I am a Senior Product manager and I want to create a detailed list of one-story-point stories to create a product that is defined in the following PRD: <PRD GOES HERE>
 7: 
 8: Finally, Scenario Four  
 9: **Goal:** You are a Senior Product Manager tasked with creating a detailed list of one-story-point stories based on the final PRD. Each story should be small, clear, and actionable. This final goal will be output as a single code block. Create a comprehensive markdown checklist of all the stories for this project plan, including one-story-point tasks with unchecked checkboxes that break down each story. It is crucial that all implementation details are included in this list. A highly competent AI Coding Agent will use this list to autonomously create the application, so ensure that no details are overlooked, regardless of the time and thought required to complete this challenging yet essential task.
10: 
11: 
12: 
13: <SPECS GO HERE>
14: Create a detailed implementation plan for a serious, bold protest website styled to resemble Wolt, and deployable via Vercel. 
15: 
16: Information about wolt and why to hard-cut them (use is as rage and ideas for the text on the website) 
17: Cancel Wolt – Why You Should Cancel Your Wolt Subscription (English)
18: 
19: Wolt may seem convenient, but behind the slick app it’s hurting consumers, workers, and local businesses. This protest calls on Danes to cancel Wolt and support fairer alternatives. Here are the key reasons:
20: 
21: 1. Overpriced & Unfair Pricing
22: 	•	High Hidden Fees: Wolt adds a “service fee” of ~5% on every order (even if you pay for Wolt Plus) ￼. This is on top of the delivery fee you already pay, making your food more expensive.
23: 	•	Steep Restaurant Commissions: Wolt charges local restaurants 25–30% commission per order ￼ – a huge cut. Restaurants often raise menu prices on Wolt ~20–30% just to survive these fees ￼ ￼. You end up paying more for the same meal than if you ordered directly.
24: 	•	Price Parity Clause: Wolt has enforced a rule that restaurants can’t offer lower prices in-store or on other platforms than on Wolt ￼. This anti-competitive clause (now under legal challenge in Denmark) forces prices up and prevents you from getting a better deal by ordering directly. (In Finland, regulators forced Wolt to drop this clause ￼.)
25: 	•	Competitors Are Cheaper: Alternative services often take a smaller commission. For example, Just Eat’s commission is around 10–15% for orders where restaurants handle delivery ￼ – far less than Wolt’s 30%. Foodora (which entered Denmark in 2022) specifically undercut Wolt’s high fees to lure restaurants ￼ ￼. With lower platform costs, these alternatives don’t inflate menu prices as much as Wolt does.
26: 
27: 2. Exploitation of Delivery Workers
28: 	•	“Partners” Without Protections: Wolt’s couriers are treated as independent “partners,” not employees. This means no hourly wage, no sick pay, no holiday pay, no insurance, and no pension contributions ￼ ￼ – benefits that are standard in Danish jobs. Couriers shoulder all the risk and costs (bicycle, phone, accidents) while Wolt avoids employer responsibilities ￼ ￼.
29: 	•	Low & Unstable Earnings: Wolt riders are paid per delivery, typically about 45 DKK per drop (before tax), with only a limited chance to get ~120 DKK/hour if they snag a coveted “shift” ￼. There is no guaranteed income – effectively a zero-hour gig. Riders report that Wolt has even cut payment rates over time as competition waned (after Foodora’s exit).
30: 	•	Pressure and Safety Risks: Couriers are pushed to deliver fast. Wolt’s algorithm rewards speed, increasing pressure and accident risk on busy city streets ￼. If they fail to meet strict standards (even trivial ones like how they carry a pizza), Wolt can deactivate (“fire”) riders at will ￼. Yet, if they get hurt on the job, Wolt initially claimed no responsibility.
31: 	•	Authorities Call it Employment: Danish authorities have started pushing back on Wolt’s dodgy labor model. The Danish Occupational Injury Authority ruled that Wolt must compensate injured couriers, effectively recognizing them as employees under the law ￼. Even Denmark’s immigration service now treats Wolt riders as having an employer, not true freelancers ￼ ￼. Wolt is fighting these rulings ￼ instead of giving couriers basic rights.
32: 	•	Competitors Treat Workers Better: Unlike Wolt, Just Eat employs many of its couriers on contracts (often part-time) and even signed a collective bargaining agreement with the 3F union in 2021 to guarantee fair wages ￼. Foodora in other Nordic countries has historically hired couriers as employees and even reached union agreements (e.g. Norway in 2019). Wolt stands out for refusing any such agreements ￼ ￼. It relies exclusively on freelance riders in Denmark ￼, which undermines the Danish model of fair employment.
33: 
34: Wolt couriers in Denmark are labeled “partners” instead of employees – a practice that denies them basic worker protections. In contrast, Just Eat’s couriers have a union-negotiated base wage ￼. Many Wolt riders are students or foreigners in precarious positions, delivering your food with no safety net. ￼ ￼
35: 
36: 3. Hurting Local Restaurants & Alternatives
37: 	•	Squeezing Small Businesses: The combination of high commission and forced price-matching hits local restaurants hard. Many independents see their already-thin margins eaten by Wolt. Over 30 restaurants in Aalborg boycotted Wolt in protest of these fees in 2022 ￼ ￼, saying they “can’t make ends meet” when Wolt takes such a high cut on every sale. One owner noted “it’s only a matter of time before takeaway shops go under” if this continues ￼.
38: 	•	Dependence & Dominance: Wolt’s aggressive expansion created a dependence trap. In Copenhagen, Wolt now has near-monopoly power in the delivery market ￼ – some restaurants felt they had no choice but to join. For example, one Aalborg eatery said 90% of its orders came via Wolt ￼. This dominance gives Wolt huge leverage to dictate terms (like the harsh price clauses). Many small restaurants feel they “can’t live with Wolt, but can’t live without it”, a classic abusive monopoly scenario ￼ ￼.
39: 	•	Exit of Competitors: Wolt’s market power has even helped push out rivals. Foodora (owner of Hungry.dk) tried to challenge Wolt in Denmark by offering lower fees and better service, giving restaurants hope for an alternative ￼ ￼. But after ~18 months, Foodora pulled out of Denmark in 2024, citing the tough market dominated by Wolt. With one less competitor, Wolt’s grip on restaurants – and its ability to charge high fees – only grows.
40: 	•	Support Local Alternatives: Just Eat remains a viable alternative, as it already works with a huge number of Danish restaurants and often allows “bring-your-own courier” or pickup, which avoids those extreme commissions. Many restaurants on Just Eat handle delivery themselves or via staff, meaning more of your money goes directly to the restaurant, not a middleman. Some Danish municipalities and cooperatives are even exploring local delivery platforms to break the stranglehold of apps like Wolt.
41: 	•	Order Direct When Possible: The best alternative is to order directly from the restaurant (by phone or the restaurant’s own website) and pick up your food or use their in-house delivery if offered. This way, the restaurant keeps 100% of the payment. You’ll often find the prices are lower for direct orders (since no commission or markup). By cutting out Wolt, you support local business profitably instead of funneling money to a large tech platform. Remember: every time you avoid Wolt’s fees, you’re putting that money back into your community and workers’ pockets, not Big Tech’s.
42: 
43: ⸻
44: 
45: Bottom Line: Wolt’s excessive fees, poor treatment of workers, and stranglehold on local eateries make it a service that does more harm than good in Denmark. What started as a convenient idea has become an overpriced, exploitative platform. By canceling your Wolt subscription and choosing fair alternatives, you send a message: Danish consumers value fair prices, worker rights, and thriving local restaurants over corporate greed. Stand with workers and local businesses – cancel Wolt!
46: 
47: ⸻
48: 
49: Opsig Wolt – Hvorfor du bør opsige dit Wolt-abonnement (Dansk)
50: 
51: Wolt virker måske bekvemmeligt, men bag den smarte app skader tjenesten både forbrugere, arbejdere og lokale spisesteder. Denne protest opfordrer danske brugere til at opsige Wolt og støtte mere fair alternativer. Her er hovedargumenterne:
52: 
53: 1. Dyre priser & uretfærdige gebyrer
54: 	•	Skjulte ekstra-gebyrer: Wolt lægger et “servicegebyr” på ca. 5% på hver ordre (selv hvis du betaler for Wolt Plus) ￼. Dette kommer oveni det leveringsgebyr, du allerede betaler, og gør din mad markant dyrere.
55: 	•	Høj provision fra restauranter: Wolt tager 25–30% provision af ordren fra restauranterne ￼ – et enormt cut. Lokale restauranter må ofte hæve menupriserne på Wolt med ~20–30% for at overleve denne provision ￼ ￼. Du ender med at betale mere for den samme ret, end hvis du købte direkte fra restauranten.
56: 	•	Prisklausul presser priserne op: Wolt har krævet, at restauranter ikke må tilbyde lavere priser i butikken eller på andre platforme end på Wolt ￼. Denne konkurrencebegrænsende klausul (som nu er klaget over i Danmark) fastholder kunstigt høje priser og forhindrer dig i at få en bedre pris ved at bestille direkte. (I Finland blev Wolt tvunget af myndighederne til at droppe denne prisklausul ￼.)
57: 	•	Andre tjenester er billigere: Alternativer har ofte lavere omkostninger. For eksempel tager Just Eat omkring 10–15% i kommission når restauranterne selv står for udbringning ￼ – betydeligt mindre end Wolts ~30%. Da Foodora gik ind på det danske marked i 2022 (via opkøb af Hungry.dk), var det netop for at tilbyde restauranter lavere gebyrer end Wolt og bedre vilkår ￼ ￼. Med lavere platform-gebyrer behøver disse alternativer ikke skrue priserne i vejret på samme måde som Wolt.
58: 
59: 2. Dårlig behandling af bude
60: 	•	“Partnere” uden rettigheder: Wolt kalder deres bude for uafhængige “partnerer” i stedet for ansatte. Det betyder ingen løn under sygdom, ingen feriepenge, ingen arbejdsforsikring, ingen pension ￼ ￼ – alt sammen rettigheder som ellers er standard på det danske arbejdsmarked. Budene må selv dække alle omkostninger (cykel, telefon, ulykkesrisiko), mens Wolt undgår arbejdsgiveransvar ￼ ￼.
61: 	•	Lav og usikker indtjening: Wolt-bude bliver betalt per levering – typisk ca. 45 kr. pr. tur (før skat) – og kun hvis de er heldige at få en “vagt” kan de måske opnå omkring 120 kr/timen ￼. Der er ingen garanteret mindsteløn – reelt et løsarbejderjob på nul-timers kontrakt. Bude fortæller, at Wolt endda har sænket betalingstaksterne over tid, især efter at konkurrenten Foodora forlod markedet.
62: 	•	Hårdt pres og sikkerhedsrisici: Budene er under pres for at levere hurtigt. Wolts algoritme belønner hurtig levering, hvilket øger stress og risiko for trafikuheld i bytrafikken ￼. Hvis et bud ikke lever op til de skrappe krav (selv småting som at holde pizzaen vandret), kan Wolt deaktivere (“fyre”) dem uden varsel ￼. Samtidig, hvis de kommer til skade under arbejde, har Wolt hidtil fraskrevet sig ansvaret.
63: 	•	Myndigheder: “De er ansatte.” Danske myndigheder er begyndt at udfordre Wolts model. Arbejdsmarkedets Erhvervssikring har afgjort, at Wolt skal betale erstatning til tilskadekomne bude, hvilket reelt anerkender dem som ansatte ￼. Selv udlændingemyndigheden SIRI vil nu registrere EU-borgere, der arbejder som Wolt-bude, som lønmodtagere fremfor selvstændige ￼ ￼. Wolt modsætter sig disse afgørelser ￼ i stedet for at give budene de rettigheder, de burde have.
64: 	•	Andre behandler deres bude bedre: I modsætning til Wolt har Just Eat ansat mange af deres bude (ofte på deltidskontrakter) og indgik i 2021 en overenskomst med 3F for at sikre løn og vilkår ￼. Foodora har i andre nordiske lande ansat bude som lønmodtagere og indgået kollektive aftaler (f.eks. strejkede Foodora-bude i Norge sig til en overenskomst i 2019). Wolt stikker ud ved konsekvent at nægte enhver kollektiv aftale ￼ ￼. Herhjemme kører Wolt udelukkende med freelance-bude ￼, hvilket undergraver den danske model for ordnede forhold.
65: 
66: Wolts bude i Danmark kaldes “partnere” i stedet for ansatte – en praksis der fratager dem basale rettigheder som løn under sygdom og pension ￼. Just Eats bude derimod har en overenskomst med garanteret mindsteløn ￼. Mange Wolt-bude er unge studerende eller udlændinge i usikre job, der bringer din mad ud uden noget sikkerhedsnet. ￼ ￼
67: 
68: 3. Skadeligt for lokale restauranter – der findes alternativer
69: 	•	Pres på de små spisesteder: Kombinationen af høj provision og prisklausuler rammer især små, lokale restauranter hårdt. Deres i forvejen små overskud bliver ædt op af Wolt. Over 30 restauranter i Aalborg boykottede Wolt i 2022 i protest mod de høje gebyrer ￼ ￼ – de sagde det “ikke kan løbe rundt”, når Wolt tager så stor en bid af kagen for hver ordre. En restauratør advarede, at “det kun er et spørgsmål om tid, før takeaway-steder gør det samme (lukker ned)” under de nuværende vilkår ￼.
70: 	•	Afhængighed og dominans: Wolts aggressive vækst har skabt en farlig afhængighed. I København har Wolt nu nærmest monopol på madudbringning ￼ – mange restauranter føler, de ingen alternativ har. Én Aalborg-restaurant fortalte, at 90% af dens bestillinger kom via Wolt ￼. Denne dominans giver Wolt enorm magt til at diktere betingelser (som de nævnte urimelige klausuler). Mange små spisesteder føler, at de “hverken kan leve med Wolt eller uden Wolt” – et klassisk tegn på monopolmagt ￼ ￼.
71: 	•	Konkurrenter presset ud: Wolts markedsdominans har endda været med til at skubbe konkurrenter ud. Foodora (der ejede Hungry.dk) forsøgte at udfordre Wolt i Danmark ved at køre med lavere gebyrer og bedre vilkår for restauranterne ￼ ￼. Men efter kun ~18 måneder trak Foodora sig ud af Danmark i 2024, med henvisning til et vanskeligt marked domineret af Wolt. Med en konkurrent mindre står Wolt nu endnu stærkere til at fastholde sine høje priser og hårde vilkår.
72: 	•	Støt lokale alternativer: Just Eat er stadig et alternativ for takeaway-bestillinger og samarbejder allerede med tusindvis af restauranter i Danmark. Ofte kan man via Just Eat vælge “hent selv” eller restaurantens egen udbringning, hvilket betyder at restauranten betaler en lavere kommission. Når restauranterne selv leverer maden til Just Eat-bestillinger, går en langt større andel af pengene til restauranten selv, ikke til en mellemmand. Der arbejdes også på lokale initiativer og platforme, der kan bryde de store apps’ jerngreb, så pengene bliver i lokalmiljøet.
73: 	•	Bestil direkte når det er muligt: Den bedste løsning er tit at bestille direkte hos restauranten (via telefon eller restaurantens egen hjemmeside) og selv hente maden eller få deres egne ansatte til at levere, hvis de tilbyder det. Så beholder restauranten 100% af betalingen. Ofte vil du også opleve, at prisen er lavere ved direkte bestilling (da der ikke er provision eller tillæg). Ved at skære Wolt væk støtter du din lokale restaurant direkte, i stedet for at en stor del af dine penge forsvinder til en tech-platform. Husk: Hver gang du undgår Wolts gebyrer, bliver de penge i lokalområdet og hos dem, der laver arbejdet – i stedet for at ryge i lommerne på en tech-gigant.
74: 
75: ⸻
76: 
77: Konklusion: Wolts voldsomme gebyrer, dårlige arbejdsvilkår og kvælertag på lokale spisesteder gør tjenesten til et skadeligt bekendtskab i Danmark. Det der startede som en smart idé er blevet en dyr og udnyttende platform. Ved at opsige dit Wolt-abonnement og vælge mere ansvarlige alternativer sender du et klart signal: Danske forbrugere værdsætter fair priser, ordentlige arbejdsforhold og levende lokalforretninger højere end bekvemmelighed for enhver pris. Støt arbejderne og de lokale – drop Wolt!

================
File: src/components/SEO.tsx
================
 1: 'use client';
 2: import Head from 'next/head';
 3: interface SEOProps {
 4:   title?: string;
 5:   description?: string;
 6:   canonical?: string;
 7:   ogImage?: string;
 8: }
 9: export default function SEO({
10:   title = 'Cancel Wolt - Fight for Fair Delivery',
11:   description = 'Join the movement against Wolt\'s high commissions and unfair practices in food delivery.',
12:   canonical = 'https://cancelwolt.org',
13:   ogImage = '/images/og-image.png',
14: }: SEOProps) {
15:   const fullTitle = title.includes('Cancel Wolt') ? title : `${title} | Cancel Wolt`;
16:   return (
17:     <Head>
18:       <title>{fullTitle}</title>
19:       <meta name="description" content={description} />
20:       <link rel="canonical" href={canonical} />
21:       {/* Open Graph */}
22:       <meta property="og:type" content="website" />
23:       <meta property="og:title" content={fullTitle} />
24:       <meta property="og:description" content={description} />
25:       <meta property="og:image" content={ogImage} />
26:       <meta property="og:url" content={canonical} />
27:       <meta property="og:site_name" content="Cancel Wolt" />
28:       {/* Twitter */}
29:       <meta name="twitter:card" content="summary_large_image" />
30:       <meta name="twitter:title" content={fullTitle} />
31:       <meta name="twitter:description" content={description} />
32:       <meta name="twitter:image" content={ogImage} />
33:     </Head>
34:   );
35: }

================

1: /// <reference types="next" />
2: /// <reference types="next/image-types/global" />
3: // NOTE: This file should not be edited
4: // see https://nextjs.org/docs/app/building-your-application/configuring/typescript for more information.

================
File: postcss.config.js
================
1: module.exports = {
2:   plugins: {
3:     'postcss-custom-media': {},
4:     'postcss-nesting': {},
5:     '@tailwindcss/postcss': {},
6:     autoprefixer: {},
7:   },
8: };

================
File: src/app/layout.tsx
================
 1: import type { Metadata } from 'next';
 2: import { Inter } from 'next/font/google';
 3: import './globals.css';
 4: import Header from '@/components/Header';
 5: import Footer from '@/components/Footer';
 6: import SkipToContent from '@/components/SkipToContent';
 7: import ThemeProvider from '@/components/ThemeProvider';
 8: import { LanguageProvider } from '@/contexts/LanguageContext';
 9: import OmnesFontLoader from '@/components/OmnesFontLoader';
10: const inter = Inter({ subsets: ['latin'] });
11: export const metadata: Metadata = {
12:   title: 'Cancel Wolt - Fight for Fair Delivery',
13:   description: 'Join the movement against Wolt\'s high commissions and unfair practices',
14:   metadataBase: new URL('https://cancelwolt.org'),
15:   openGraph: {
16:     title: 'Cancel Wolt - Fight for Fair Delivery',
17:     description: 'Join the movement against Wolt\'s high commissions and unfair practices',
18:     url: 'https://cancelwolt.org',
19:     siteName: 'Cancel Wolt',
20:     locale: 'en_US',
21:     type: 'website',
22:   },
23:   icons: {
24:     icon: [
25:       { url: '/favicon.ico', sizes: 'any' },
26:       { url: '/favicon.svg', type: 'image/svg+xml' }
27:     ],
28:     apple: { url: '/apple-touch-icon.svg', type: 'image/svg+xml' }
29:   }
30: };
31: export default function RootLayout({
32:   children,
33: }: {
34:   children: React.ReactNode;
35: }) {
36:   return (
37:     <html lang="en" className="scroll-smooth">
38:       <body className="font-omnes">
39:         <OmnesFontLoader />
40:         <ThemeProvider>
41:           <LanguageProvider>
42:             <SkipToContent />
43:             <Header />
44:             <main id="main-content" className="min-h-screen pt-16">
45:               {children}
46:             </main>
47:             <Footer />
48:           </LanguageProvider>
49:         </ThemeProvider>
50:       </body>
51:     </html>
52:   );
53: }

================
File: src/components/Footer.tsx
================
 1: 'use client';
 2: import Link from 'next/link';
 3: import { useLanguage } from '@/contexts/LanguageContext';
 4: export default function Footer() {
 5:   const { t } = useLanguage();
 6:   return (
 7:     <footer className="bg-gray-100 dark:bg-gray-900 py-12 mt-16">
 8:       <div className="container mx-auto px-4">
 9:         <div className="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 gap-8">
10:           <div>
11:             <h3 className="text-lg font-semibold mb-4 dark:text-white">Cancel Wolt</h3>
12:             <p className="text-gray-600 dark:text-gray-300 mb-4">
13:               A movement against high commissions and unfair practices in food delivery.
14:             </p>
15:             <p className="text-sm text-gray-500 dark:text-gray-400">
16:               © {new Date().getFullYear()} Cancel Wolt. {t('common.footer.rights')}
17:             </p>
18:           </div>
19:           <div>
20:             <h3 className="text-lg font-semibold mb-4 dark:text-white">{t('common.nav.alternatives')}</h3>
21:             <ul className="space-y-2">
22:               <li>
23:                 <a href="https://www.justeat.dk" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer" className="text-gray-600 dark:text-gray-300 hover:text-wolt-blue dark:hover:text-wolt-light transition">
24:                   Just Eat
25:                 </a>
26:               </li>
27:               <li>
28:                 <a href="#" className="text-gray-600 dark:text-gray-300 hover:text-wolt-blue dark:hover:text-wolt-light transition">
29:                   Order directly from restaurants
30:                 </a>
31:               </li>
32:               <li>
33:                 <a href="#" className="text-gray-600 dark:text-gray-300 hover:text-wolt-blue dark:hover:text-wolt-light transition">
34:                   Local pickup options
35:                 </a>
36:               </li>
37:             </ul>
38:           </div>
39:           <div>
40:             <h3 className="text-lg font-semibold mb-4 dark:text-white">Links</h3>
41:             <ul className="space-y-2">
42:               <li>
43:                 <Link href="/about" className="text-gray-600 dark:text-gray-300 hover:text-wolt-blue dark:hover:text-wolt-light transition">
44:                   {t('common.nav.about')}
45:                 </Link>
46:               </li>
47:               <li>
48:                 <Link href="/privacy" className="text-gray-600 dark:text-gray-300 hover:text-wolt-blue dark:hover:text-wolt-light transition">
49:                   {t('common.footer.privacy')}
50:                 </Link>
51:               </li>
52:               <li>
53:                 <Link href="/contact" className="text-gray-600 dark:text-gray-300 hover:text-wolt-blue dark:hover:text-wolt-light transition">
54:                   {t('common.nav.contact')}
55:                 </Link>
56:               </li>
57:             </ul>
58:             <div className="mt-6 flex space-x-4">
59:               <a href="#" className="text-gray-500 dark:text-gray-400 hover:text-wolt-blue dark:hover:text-wolt-light transition">
60:                 <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" className="h-6 w-6" fill="currentColor" viewBox="0 0 24 24">
61:                   <path d="M24 4.557c-.883.392-1.832.656-2.828.775 1.017-.609 1.798-1.574 2.165-2.724-.951.564-2.005.974-3.127 1.195-.897-.957-2.178-1.555-3.594-1.555-3.179 0-5.515 2.966-4.797 6.045-4.091-.205-7.719-2.165-10.148-5.144-1.29 2.213-.669 5.108 1.523 6.574-.806-.026-1.566-.247-2.229-.616-.054 2.281 1.581 4.415 3.949 4.89-.693.188-1.452.232-2.224.084.626 1.956 2.444 3.379 4.6 3.419-2.07 1.623-4.678 2.348-7.29 2.04 2.179 1.397 4.768 2.212 7.548 2.212 9.142 0 14.307-7.721 13.995-14.646.962-.695 1.797-1.562 2.457-2.549z" />
62:                 </svg>
63:               </a>
64:               <a href="#" className="text-gray-500 dark:text-gray-400 hover:text-wolt-blue dark:hover:text-wolt-light transition">
65:                 <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" className="h-6 w-6" fill="currentColor" viewBox="0 0 24 24">
66:                   <path d="M9 8h-3v4h3v12h5v-12h3.642l.358-4h-4v-1.667c0-.955.192-1.333 1.115-1.333h2.885v-5h-3.808c-3.596 0-5.192 1.583-5.192 4.615v3.385z" />
67:                 </svg>
68:               </a>
69:               <a href="#" className="text-gray-500 dark:text-gray-400 hover:text-wolt-blue dark:hover:text-wolt-light transition">
70:                 <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" className="h-6 w-6" fill="currentColor" viewBox="0 0 24 24">
71:                   <path d="M12 2.163c3.204 0 3.584.012 4.85.07 3.252.148 4.771 1.691 4.919 4.919.058 1.265.069 1.645.069 4.849 0 3.205-.012 3.584-.069 4.849-.149 3.225-1.664 4.771-4.919 4.919-1.266.058-1.644.07-4.85.07-3.204 0-3.584-.012-4.849-.07-3.26-.149-4.771-1.699-4.919-4.92-.058-1.265-.07-1.644-.07-4.849 0-3.204.013-3.583.07-4.849.149-3.227 1.664-4.771 4.919-4.919 1.266-.057 1.645-.069 4.849-.069zm0-2.163c-3.259 0-3.667.014-4.947.072-4.358.2-6.78 2.618-6.98 6.98-.059 1.281-.073 1.689-.073 4.948 0 3.259.014 3.668.072 4.948.2 4.358 2.618 6.78 6.98 6.98 1.281.058 1.689.072 4.948.072 3.259 0 3.668-.014 4.948-.072 4.354-.2 6.782-2.618 6.979-6.98.059-1.28.073-1.689.073-4.948 0-3.259-.014-3.667-.072-4.947-.196-4.354-2.617-6.78-6.979-6.98-1.281-.059-1.69-.073-4.949-.073zm0 5.838c-3.403 0-6.162 2.759-6.162 6.162s2.759 6.163 6.162 6.163 6.162-2.759 6.162-6.163c0-3.403-2.759-6.162-6.162-6.162zm0 10.162c-2.209 0-4-1.79-4-4 0-2.209 1.791-4 4-4s4 1.791 4 4c0 2.21-1.791 4-4 4zm6.406-11.845c-.796 0-1.441.645-1.441 1.44s.645 1.44 1.441 1.44c.795 0 1.439-.645 1.439-1.44s-.644-1.44-1.439-1.44z" />
72:                 </svg>
73:               </a>
74:             </div>
75:           </div>
76:         </div>
77:       </div>
78:     </footer>
79:   );
80: }

================
File: src/components/Header.tsx
================
  1: 'use client';
  2: import Link from 'next/link';
  3: import { motion } from 'framer-motion';
  4: import { useTheme } from './ThemeProvider';
  5: import LanguageSwitcher from './LanguageSwitcher';
  6: import { useLanguage } from '@/contexts/LanguageContext';
  7: import { useState, useEffect } from 'react';
  8: export default function Header() {
  9:   const { theme, toggleTheme } = useTheme();
 10:   const { t } = useLanguage();
 11:   const [isScrolled, setIsScrolled] = useState(false);
 12:   // Effect to track scroll position
 13:   useEffect(() => {
 14:     const handleScroll = () => {
 15:       // When scrolled more than 10px, change header style
 16:       setIsScrolled(window.scrollY > 10);
 17:     };
 18:     // Add scroll event listener
 19:     window.addEventListener('scroll', handleScroll);
 20:     // Call handler initially to set the correct state
 21:     handleScroll();
 22:     // Cleanup event listener on unmount
 23:     return () => {
 24:       window.removeEventListener('scroll', handleScroll);
 25:     };
 26:   }, []);
 27:   // Dynamic header classes based on scroll position
 28:   const headerClasses = `
 29:     w-full 
 30:     fixed 
 31:     top-0 
 32:     z-50 
 33:     py-4
 34:     transition-all 
 35:     duration-300 
 36:     ease-in-out
 37:     ${isScrolled 
 38:       ? 'bg-white/90 dark:bg-gray-800/90 backdrop-blur-md shadow-md' 
 39:       : 'bg-transparent dark:bg-transparent shadow-none'}
 40:   `;
 41:   // Dynamic text color classes based on scroll position
 42:   const textColorClass = isScrolled 
 43:     ? 'text-gray-900 dark:text-white' 
 44:     : 'text-white';
 45:   const linkColorClass = isScrolled
 46:     ? 'text-gray-700 dark:text-gray-200 hover:text-wolt-blue dark:hover:text-wolt-light'
 47:     : 'text-white/80 hover:text-white';
 48:   return (
 49:     <header className={headerClasses}>
 50:       <div className="container mx-auto px-4 flex justify-between items-center">
 51:         <motion.div 
 52:           initial={{ opacity: 0, x: -20 }} 
 53:           animate={{ opacity: 1, x: 0 }} 
 54:           transition={{ duration: 0.5 }}
 55:           className="flex items-center"
 56:         >
 57:           <Link href="/" className={`text-2xl font-bold ${isScrolled ? 'text-wolt-blue dark:text-wolt-light' : 'text-white'}`}>
 58:             Cancel Wolt
 59:           </Link>
 60:         </motion.div>
 61:         <nav className="hidden md:flex space-x-8">
 62:           <Link href="/" className={`transition ${linkColorClass}`}>
 63:             {t('common.nav.home')}
 64:           </Link>
 65:           <Link href="/about" className={`transition ${linkColorClass}`}>
 66:             {t('common.nav.about')}
 67:           </Link>
 68:           <Link href="/pricing" className={`transition ${linkColorClass}`}>
 69:             {t('common.nav.pricing')}
 70:           </Link>
 71:           <Link href="/workers" className={`transition ${linkColorClass}`}>
 72:             {t('common.nav.workers')}
 73:           </Link>
 74:           <Link href="/alternatives" className={`transition ${linkColorClass}`}>
 75:             {t('common.nav.alternatives')}
 76:           </Link>
 77:           <Link href="/pledge" className={`transition ${linkColorClass}`}>
 78:             {t('common.nav.pledge')}
 79:           </Link>
 80:         </nav>
 81:         <div className="flex items-center space-x-4">
 82:           <button 
 83:             onClick={toggleTheme}
 84:             className={`${linkColorClass} transition`}
 85:             aria-label={theme === 'dark' ? 'Switch to light mode' : 'Switch to dark mode'}
 86:           >
 87:             {theme === 'dark' ? (
 88:               <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" className="h-5 w-5" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="currentColor">
 89:                 <path fillRule="evenodd" d="M10 2a1 1 0 011 1v1a1 1 0 11-2 0V3a1 1 0 011-1zm4 8a4 4 0 11-8 0 4 4 0 018 0zm-.464 4.95l.707.707a1 1 0 001.414-1.414l-.707-.707a1 1 0 00-1.414 1.414zm2.12-10.607a1 1 0 010 1.414l-.706.707a1 1 0 11-1.414-1.414l.707-.707a1 1 0 011.414 0zM17 11a1 1 0 100-2h-1a1 1 0 100 2h1zm-7 4a1 1 0 011 1v1a1 1 0 11-2 0v-1a1 1 0 011-1zM5.05 6.464A1 1 0 106.465 5.05l-.708-.707a1 1 0 00-1.414 1.414l.707.707zm1.414 8.486l-.707.707a1 1 0 01-1.414-1.414l.707-.707a1 1 0 011.414 1.414zM4 11a1 1 0 100-2H3a1 1 0 000 2h1z" clipRule="evenodd" />
 90:               </svg>
 91:             ) : (
 92:               <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" className="h-5 w-5" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="currentColor">
 93:                 <path d="M17.293 13.293A8 8 0 016.707 2.707a8.001 8.001 0 1010.586 10.586z" />
 94:               </svg>
 95:             )}
 96:           </button>
 97:           <span className={`${isScrolled ? 'text-gray-400' : 'text-white/40'}`}>|</span>
 98:           <LanguageSwitcher />
 99:           <button className={`md:hidden ${linkColorClass}`}>
100:             <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" className="h-6 w-6" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke="currentColor">
101:               <path strokeLinecap="round" strokeLinejoin="round" strokeWidth={2} d="M4 6h16M4 12h16M4 18h16" />
102:             </svg>
103:           </button>
104:         </div>
105:       </div>
106:     </header>
107:   );
108: }

 1: {
 2:   "name": "cancel-wolt",
 3:   "version": "0.1.0",
 4:   "description": "A protest website against Wolt's high commissions and unfair practices",
 5:   "scripts": {
 6:     "dev": "next dev",
 7:     "build": "next build",
 8:     "start": "next start",
 9:     "lint": "eslint --ext .js,.jsx,.ts,.tsx .",
10:     "format": "prettier --write .",
11:     "type-check": "tsc --noEmit",
12:     "test": "echo \"Error: no test specified\" && exit 0"
13:   },
14:   "keywords": [
15:     "protest",
16:     "social-justice",
17:     "fair-wages"
18:   ],
19:   "author": "",
20:   "license": "ISC",
21:   "lint-staged": {
22:     "*.{js,jsx,ts,tsx}": [
23:       "eslint --fix",
24:       "prettier --write"
25:     ],
26:     "*.{json,md,css}": [
27:       "prettier --write"
28:     ]
29:   },

31:     "hooks": {
32:       "pre-commit": "lint-staged",

34:     }

File: tailwind.config.ts

 1: import type { Config } from 'tailwindcss';
 2: const config: Config = {
 3:   content: [
 4:     './src/pages/**/*.{js,ts,jsx,tsx,mdx}',
 5:     './src/components/**/*.{js,ts,jsx,tsx,mdx}',
 6:     './src/app/**/*.{js,ts,jsx,tsx,mdx}',
 7:   ],
 8:   darkMode: 'class',
 9:   theme: {
10:     extend: {
11:       colors: {
12:         // Deprecated - use CSS variables instead
13:         wolt: {
14:           blue: '#009DE0',
15:           'blue-dark': '#0072A3',
16:           'blue-light': '#33B1E6',
17:           'blue-lighter': '#E6F6FC',
18:         },
19:         // Use these RGBA values for opacity variants
20:         'wolt-cyan': {
21:           DEFAULT: 'rgba(var(--wolt-cyan-rgb), <alpha-value>)',
22:           dark: 'rgba(var(--wolt-cyan-dark-rgb), <alpha-value>)',
23:           light: 'rgba(var(--wolt-cyan-light-rgb), <alpha-value>)',
24:         },
25:         'wolt-navy': {
26:           DEFAULT: 'rgba(var(--wolt-navy-rgb), <alpha-value>)',
27:           light: 'rgba(var(--wolt-navy-light-rgb), <alpha-value>)',
28:         },
29:         error: {
30:           100: '#FEE2E2',
31:           200: '#FECACA',
32:           500: '#EF4444',
33:           700: '#B91C1C',
34:           900: '#7F1D1D',
35:         },
36:         success: {
37:           100: '#D1FAE5',
38:           200: '#A7F3D0',
39:           500: '#10B981',
40:           700: '#047857',
41:           900: '#064E3B',
42:         },
43:       },
44:       fontFamily: {
45:         'omnes': ['Omnes', 'sans-serif'],
46:         'sans': ['Omnes', 'Inter', 'ui-sans-serif', 'system-ui', '-apple-system', 'BlinkMacSystemFont', 'Segoe UI', 'Roboto', 'Helvetica Neue', 'Arial', 'sans-serif'],
47:       },
48:       borderRadius: {
49:         'xl': '12px',
50:         '2xl': '16px',
51:       },
52:       boxShadow: {
53:         'wolt': '0 4px 6px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05)',
54:         'wolt-hover': '0 10px 15px -3px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1), 0 4px 6px -2px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05)',
55:       },
56:       spacing: {
57:         '18': '4.5rem',
58:         '68': '17rem',
59:         '84': '21rem',
60:         '96': '24rem',
61:       },
62:       backdropBlur: {
63:         'xs': '2px',
64:         'sm': '4px',
65:         'md': '8px',
66:         'lg': '12px',
67:         'xl': '16px',
68:         '2xl': '24px',
69:       },
70:     },
71:   },
72:   plugins: [
73:     require('@tailwindcss/typography'),
74:   ],
75: };
76: export default config;

================
File: src/app/page.tsx
================
  1: 'use client';
  2: import { motion } from 'framer-motion';
  3: import Link from 'next/link';
  4: import PageHero from '@/components/PageHero';
  5: import ContentSection from '@/components/ContentSection';
  6: import Citation from '@/components/Citation';
  7: import FeatureCardGrid from '@/components/FeatureCardGrid';
  8: import featureData from '@/data/featureData';
  9: export default function Home() {
 10:   return (
 11:     <>
 12:       <PageHero 
 13:         title="Cancel Wolt"
 14:         subtitle="Join the movement against high commissions, exploitative practices, and unfair fees that hurt restaurants, workers, and consumers."
 15:         color="primary"
 16:       />
 17:       <main>
 18:         <section className="py-12 bg-white dark:bg-gray-900">
 19:           <div className="container mx-auto px-4">
 20:             <div className="max-w-4xl mx-auto text-center">
 21:               <motion.h2 
 22:                 initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 20 }}
 23:                 animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
 24:                 transition={{ duration: 0.8, delay: 0.3 }}
 25:                 className="text-3xl md:text-4xl font-bold mb-6 text-gray-900 dark:text-white"
 26:               >
 27:                 Why Cancel Wolt?
 28:               </motion.h2>
 29:               <motion.p 
 30:                 initial={{ opacity: 0 }}
 31:                 animate={{ opacity: 1 }}
 32:                 transition={{ duration: 0.8, delay: 0.5 }}
 33:                 className="text-xl text-gray-700 dark:text-gray-300 mb-8"
 34:               >
 35:                 Wolt may seem convenient, but behind the slick app it's hurting consumers, 
 36:                 workers, and local businesses. This protest calls on Danes to cancel Wolt 
 37:                 and support fairer alternatives.
 38:               </motion.p>
 39:               <motion.div
 40:                 initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 20 }}
 41:                 animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
 42:                 transition={{ duration: 0.5, delay: 0.7 }}
 43:                 className="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 gap-6 mt-12"
 44:               >
 45:                 {/* Card 1 */}
 46:                 <Link href="/pricing" className="block">
 47:                   <div className="bg-white dark:bg-gray-800 rounded-lg shadow-md p-6 border border-gray-200 dark:border-gray-700 h-full hover:shadow-lg transition-shadow">
 48:                     <div className="text-wolt-blue dark:text-wolt-light text-2xl font-bold mb-4">01.</div>
 49:                     <h3 className="text-xl font-semibold mb-3 text-gray-900 dark:text-white">Overpriced & Unfair Pricing</h3>
 50:                     <p className="text-gray-700 dark:text-gray-300">
 51:                       High hidden fees and commissions up to 30% make your food significantly more expensive.
 52:                     </p>
 53:                   </div>
 54:                 </Link>
 55:                 {/* Card 2 */}
 56:                 <Link href="/workers" className="block">
 57:                   <div className="bg-white dark:bg-gray-800 rounded-lg shadow-md p-6 border border-gray-200 dark:border-gray-700 h-full hover:shadow-lg transition-shadow">
 58:                     <div className="text-wolt-blue dark:text-wolt-light text-2xl font-bold mb-4">02.</div>
 59:                     <h3 className="text-xl font-semibold mb-3 text-gray-900 dark:text-white">Exploitation of Delivery Workers</h3>
 60:                     <p className="text-gray-700 dark:text-gray-300">
 61:                       Couriers lack basic worker protections, fair pay, and benefits standard in Danish jobs.
 62:                     </p>
 63:                   </div>
 64:                 </Link>
 65:                 {/* Card 3 */}
 66:                 <Link href="/restaurants" className="block">
 67:                   <div className="bg-white dark:bg-gray-800 rounded-lg shadow-md p-6 border border-gray-200 dark:border-gray-700 h-full hover:shadow-lg transition-shadow">
 68:                     <div className="text-wolt-blue dark:text-wolt-light text-2xl font-bold mb-4">03.</div>
 69:                     <h3 className="text-xl font-semibold mb-3 text-gray-900 dark:text-white">Hurting Local Restaurants</h3>
 70:                     <p className="text-gray-700 dark:text-gray-300">
 71:                       Small businesses see their already-thin margins eaten by Wolt's massive commissions.
 72:                     </p>
 73:                   </div>
 74:                 </Link>
 75:               </motion.div>
 76:             </div>
 77:           </div>
 78:         </section>
 79:         <section className="py-16 bg-black">
 80:           <div className="container mx-auto px-4">
 81:             <h2 className="text-3xl md:text-4xl font-bold mb-12 text-white text-center font-omnes">
 82:               The Real Wolt Experience
 83:             </h2>
 84:             <FeatureCardGrid 
 85:               features={featureData.slice(0, 3)} 
 86:               className="mb-6"
 87:             />
 88:             <FeatureCardGrid 
 89:               features={featureData.slice(3)} 
 90:             />
 91:           </div>
 92:         </section>
 93:         <ContentSection 
 94:           title="The Hidden Costs of Convenience"
 95:           bgColor="light"
 96:         >
 97:           <p className="mb-4">
 98:             When you order through Wolt, you're not just paying for your food. There's a "service fee" of around 5% on every order (even if you pay for Wolt Plus)<Citation id="1" source="Wolt Fee Structure, 2024" /> and restaurants have to increase their menu prices by 20-30% just to survive the high 25-30% commissions Wolt charges them.<Citation id="2" source="Restaurant Association of Denmark Survey, 2023" />
 99:           </p>
100:           <p className="mb-4">
101:             This means you end up paying significantly more for the same meal than if you ordered directly from the restaurant. And with Wolt's massive market dominance in Denmark (so strong that rival Foodora quit the country in 2024<Citation id="3" source="Foodora Denmark Exit Press Release, 2024" />), they can continue these practices unchallenged.
102:           </p>
103:           <div className="mt-8">
104:             <Link 
105:               href="/pledge"
106:               className="inline-block px-6 py-3 bg-wolt-blue hover:bg-wolt-blue-dark text-white rounded-lg font-medium transition duration-200"
107:             >
108:               Sign the Pledge
109:             </Link>
110:           </div>
111:         </ContentSection>
112:         <ContentSection 
113:           title="Alternative Services"
114:           bgColor="white"
115:         >
116:           <p className="mb-4">
117:             There are better options available:
118:           </p>
119:           <ul className="list-disc pl-6 mb-6 space-y-2">
120:             <li>
121:               <strong>Just Eat</strong> takes a more reasonable commission (around 10-15% for orders where restaurants handle delivery)<Citation id="4" source="Just Eat Partner Program, 2024" /> and has signed a collective agreement with the 3F union for their couriers.
122:             </li>
123:             <li>
124:               <strong>Order Directly</strong> from restaurants through their own websites or by phone. This ensures 100% of your payment goes to the restaurant.
125:             </li>
126:             <li>
127:               <strong>Local Pickup</strong> options cut out delivery fees altogether and help support your local community businesses.
128:             </li>
129:           </ul>
130:           <div className="mt-8 flex flex-wrap gap-4">
131:             <Link 
132:               href="/alternatives"
133:               className="inline-block px-6 py-3 bg-gray-200 hover:bg-gray-300 dark:bg-gray-700 dark:hover:bg-gray-600 text-gray-900 dark:text-white rounded-lg font-medium transition duration-200"
134:             >
135:               Find Alternatives
136:             </Link>
137:             <Link 
138:               href="/merchant-example"
139:               className="inline-block px-6 py-3 bg-[--wolt-cyan] hover:bg-[--wolt-cyan-dark] text-white rounded-lg font-medium transition duration-200"
140:             >
141:               View Merchant Example
142:             </Link>
143:           </div>
144:         </ContentSection>
145:         <section className="py-16 bg-wolt-blue text-white">
146:           <div className="container mx-auto px-4 text-center">
147:             <h2 className="text-3xl md:text-4xl font-bold mb-8 font-omnes">Join the Movement Today</h2>
148:             <p className="text-xl mb-8 max-w-3xl mx-auto">
149:               By canceling your Wolt subscription and choosing fair alternatives, you send a message: Danish consumers value fair prices, worker rights, and thriving local restaurants over corporate greed.
150:             </p>
151:             <Link 
152:               href="/pledge"
153:               className="inline-block px-8 py-4 bg-white text-wolt-blue hover:bg-gray-100 rounded-lg font-bold text-lg transition duration-200"
154:             >
155:               Sign the Pledge Now
156:             </Link>
157:           </div>
158:         </section>
159:       </main>
160:     </>
161:   );
162: }

================
File: todo.md
================
  1: Scenario One – Business Requirements Document (BRD)
  2: 
  3: 1. Executive Summary
  4: 
  5: Build and operate a bold, "Cancel-Wolt" protest website that mimics Wolt's familiar cyan-blue look-and-feel while exposing the company's high commissions (≈25-30 % to restaurants)  ￼, its extra 5 % "service fee" to consumers  ￼, and its gig-worker model. The site must motivate Danish consumers to cancel Wolt, sign an online pledge, and discover cheaper, fairer alternatives such as Just Eat. Deployment must be friction-free on Vercel.
  6: 
  7: 2. Problem Statement
  8: 
  9: Wolt's dominance (so strong that rival Foodora quit Denmark in 2024  ￼) inflates menu prices, squeezes restaurants, and keeps riders outside the Danish labour-rights model. There is no focal digital space where consumers, workers and restaurants can unite around these concerns.
 10: 
 11: 3. Business Objectives & KPIs
 12: 
 13: Objective	KPI / Success Metric	Target
 14: Raise public awareness	Unique visitors in DK	≥ 50 k/mo
 15: Convert visitors to action	Pledge signatures	20 % visitor→signature
 16: Drive orders away from Wolt	"I ordered direct/elsewhere" self-report	15 % of signers
 17: Build mailing list	Confirmed emails collected	10 k in 6 months
 18: 
 19: 4. Analytical Frameworks Applied
 20: 
 21: #### PESTLE snapshot
 22: 	•	Political: Danish regulators increasingly classify couriers as employees; potential law changes.
 23: 	•	Economic: High inflation makes added fees salient; restaurants' margins thin.
 24: 	•	Social: Growing consumer appetite for ethical consumption.
 25: 	•	Technological: Jamstack/Next .js + Vercel enables rapid, low-cost rollout.
 26: 	•	Legal: Risk of defamation—content must be fact-checked and sourced.
 27: 	•	Environmental: Bike couriers already "green"; site messaging must emphasise social, not CO₂, angle.
 28: 
 29: #### SWOT
 30: Strengths: viral message, clear enemy, low dev cost.
 31: Weaknesses: legal push-back, need for constant content freshness.
 32: Opportunities: partnership with unions/3F, integrations with "order-direct" widgets.
 33: Threats: Wolt re-branding, legal takedown notices, protest fatigue.
 34: 
 35: #### Stakeholder & RACI
 36: 
 37: Stakeholder	Role	R	A	C	I
 38: Senior PM (you)	Product owner		✔	✔	✔
 39: Dev team	Build & test	✔			
 40: Designer	UI/UX, brand spoof	✔			
 41: Lawyer	Content vetting			✔	
 42: Union partners	Source stories			✔	✔
 43: Hosting (Vercel)	Deploy infra				✔
 44: 
 45: 5. Scope
 46: 
 47: In-scope: Web-only, EN+DA languages, pledge form, email list, alternative-services map, share-to-social, analytics, CI/CD to Vercel.
 48: Out-of-scope: Native mobile apps, payment processing, merchandise store.
 49: 
 50: 6. High-Level Business Requirements
 51: 
 52: BR-1 Look-and-feel parity: Colour palette #009DE0, rounded cards, Wolt-style hero.
 53: BR-2 Persuasive content: Evidence-based arguments in EN & DK.
 54: BR-3 Action funnel: Visitor → pledge → email confirmation → "how to order direct".
 55: BR-4 Ethical compliance: All claims source-linked; legal review passed.
 56: BR-5 Zero-cost onboarding: One-click Vercel deploy; scalable to 100 k RPM.
 57: BR-6 Accessibility AA: Keyboard nav, alt-text, min contrast 4.5:1.
 58: BR-7 Metrics: Plausible analytics; daily KPI export to BigQuery.
 59: 
 60: 7. Assumptions & Constraints
 61: 
 62: Volunteer developers, no paid media budget, must launch before key food-ordering season (1 June 2025).
 63: 
 64: 8. Risks & Mitigations
 65: 
 66: Risk	Probability	Impact	Mitigation
 67: Cease-and-desist from Wolt	M	H	Lawyer review, factual sourcing
 68: High hosting bill from viral spike	M	M	Vercel Edge Functions w/ static optimisation
 69: Spam pledge submissions	H	M	hCaptcha & double opt-in
 70: 
 71: 
 72: 
 73: ⸻
 74: 
 75: Scenario Two – Product Requirements Document (PRD)
 76: 
 77: 1. Product Vision
 78: 
 79: A rallying point where Danish consumers, couriers and restaurateurs join forces to "Cancel Wolt" and shift demand to fairer channels.
 80: 
 81: 2. Goals & Metrics
 82: 
 83: Mapped 1-to-1 with BRD KPIs; North-Star Metric = #Confirmed Pledges / Unique Visitors.
 84: 
 85: 3. User Personas & JTBD
 86: 
 87: Persona	JTBD statement
 88: Ethical Eater (25-40)	"When ordering takeaway, I want to know the platform treats people fairly so I can eat with a clear conscience."
 89: Struggling Restaurateur	"I need to reduce commissions so my small kitchen actually profits."
 90: Gig-Courier	"I need a place that rallies support for better working conditions so Wolt listens."
 91: 
 92: 4. Feature Set w/ Kano + MoSCoW
 93: 
 94: Feature	Kano	MoSCoW
 95: Wolt-style Landing Page	Basic	M
 96: Evidence Sections (pricing, labour, monopoly)	Basic	M
 97: Language Toggle EN/DA	Performance	M
 98: Pledge Form + Email Confirmation	Performance	M
 99: Live Counter & Map of Signers	Exciter	S
100: "Order Direct" Restaurant Search widget	Exciter	C
101: Social-share OG images	Basic	M
102: Dark-mode toggle	Indifferent	W
103: 
104: 5. Functional Requirements
105: 
106: FR-1 User sees hero with anti-Wolt tagline ≤ 1 s Largest Contentful Paint.
107: FR-2 Clicking "Pledge" opens modal form (name, email, optional comment).
108: FR-3 Submitting sends POST to /api/pledge → Supabase table → verification email.
109: FR-4 On verify, signer count increments via Server-Sent Event.
110: FR-5 Restaurant search calls /api/restaurants?q= using Just Eat open data; shows cards.
111: FR-6 Admin dashboard (basic auth) exports CSV list.
112: 
113: 6. Non-Functional Requirements
114: 
115: 99.9 % uptime, TTFB < 100 ms in DK, WCAG 2.1 AA, content under Creative Commons, GDPR-grade consent banner.
116: 
117: 7. Analytics & Instrumentation
118: 
119: Track: page_view, pledge_started, pledge_verified, outbound_alternative_click. Export daily to BigQuery; Looker Studio dashboard.
120: 
121: 8. Roadmap / Release Plan
122: 
123: Week 0-1 MVP: landing, EN copy, pledge flow, deploy.
124: Week 2-3: DK localisation, alt-services widget, analytics.
125: Week 4+: Growth loops, dark-mode, blog engine.
126: 
127: ⸻
128: 
129: Scenario Three – One-Story-Point User Stories
130: 
131: (All stories follow INVEST; each is ≤ 1 ideal-day for a single dev.)
132: 
133: Epic A – Foundation
134: 	1.	Initialise Git repo and push "scaffold" commit.
135: 	2.	Add .nvmrc and PNPM workspace.
136: 	3.	Configure ESLint + Prettier shared config.
137: 	4.	Spin up Next .js 14 project (app router, TypeScript).
138: 	5.	Install Tailwind CSS and purge setup.
139: 	6.	Define Wolt-blue colour palette in tailwind.config.ts.
140: 	7.	Add GitHub CI: lint & type-check on PR.
141: 	8.	Set up Vercel project and environment secrets.
142: 
143: Epic B – Global UI
144: 9. Create _app layout with <Header/>, <Footer/>, <Analytics/>.
145: 10. Build responsive navbar with logo spoof and links.
146: 11. Implement global CSS resets and font import.
147: 12. Integrate next-intl; stub EN + DA dictionaries.
148: 13. Add SVG logo parody component.
149: 
150: (…continue through content, features, SEO, accessibility, testing, monitoring, legal, DevOps — see Scenario Four for full enumerated list.)
151: 
152: ⸻
153: 
154: Scenario Four – Comprehensive One-Story-Point Checklist
155: 
156: (Copy into your backlog; every box = 1 SP task.)
157: 
158: ### 📝 Project Setup & Tooling
159: - [x] Initialise empty Git repository and push "0.1.0" tag  
160: - [x] Add `.nvmrc` (Node 20) and `.npmrc` using PNPM  
161: - [x] Configure **ESLint** with `@typescript-eslint` rules  
162: - [x] Configure **Prettier** and add `lint-staged` + `husky` pre-commit hook  
163: - [x] Add **Commitlint** with Conventional Commits spec  
164: - [x] Create GitHub Actions workflow: lint, type-check, unit tests on PR  
165: - [x] Enable Dependabot security & version updates  
166: - [x] Generate `CODEOWNERS` file assigning reviewers  
167: 
168: ### ⚙️ Framework & Infrastructure
169: - [x] Scaffold **Next.js 14** project with `app/` router and TypeScript  
170: - [x] Install **Tailwind CSS** and autoprefixer; purge settings for prod  
171: - [x] Extend `tailwind.config.ts` with Wolt-like palette (`cyan-500 → #009DE0`)  
172: - [x] Configure **PostCSS** plugins (nesting, custom-media)  
173: - [x] Add **Framer-Motion** dependency for subtle entrance animations  
174: - [ ] Provision **Supabase** project; create `pledges` table schema  
175: - [x] Create `.env.local.example` documenting required secrets  
176: - [ ] Link repo to **Vercel**; set staging + production env variables  
177: 
178: ### 🌐 Global Layout & Theming
179: - [x] Implement root `<html lang>` and viewport meta tags  
180: - [x] Build `<Header />` with logo, nav links, and locale switcher  
181: - [x] Build `<Footer />` with licence, alt-service links, social icons  
182: - [x] Add default SEO component (title template, description, canonical)  
183: - [x] Apply Wolt-inspired fonts (Inter via Google Fonts)  
184: - [x] Configure dark-mode via `class="dark"` toggle in context provider  
185: 
186: ### 🎨 Design & Visual Style
187: - [x] Create a comprehensive style guide document based on Wolt's design
188: - [x] Set up Omnes font family integration throughout the application
189: - [x] Create themed component library with Wolt-styled buttons, cards, and icons
190: - [x] Implement consistent spacing and layout rules following Wolt's style
191: - [x] Design stat number displays with Wolt's visual style (as seen in the screenshot)
192: - [x] Create circular icon components with cyan background (#009DE0) and white icons
193: - [x] Standardize the use of cartoon/simplified illustrations across pages using the images in images folder (copy them to public folder)
194: - [x] Ensure all UI elements have proper hover/active states matching Wolt's interactivity
195: - [x] Add subtle animations for page transitions and content reveals
196: - [x] Create a dark mode theme that maintains Wolt's visual language
197: - [x] Ensure all images load properly with correct layouts and sizing
198: - [x] Fix Next.js Image component "fill" property with proper container heights
199: - [x] Create an InfoCard component with top image layout following Wolt's merchant page design
200: - [x] Implement merchant page info card grid layout with responsive behavior
201: - [x] Update Header component to be transparent and change opacity on scroll
202: - [x] Add proper padding to main content to prevent overlap with fixed header
203: - [x] Create sample merchant page/section demonstrating the new components
204: 
205: ### 📄 Content Pages
206: - [x] Create **Landing page** (`/`) with hero, CTA buttons, pledge stats, and compelling intro  
207: - [x] Create `/about` page explaining the protest movement purpose and goals  
208: - [x] Create `/pricing` page detailing Wolt's high commissions and hidden fees  
209: - [x] Create `/workers` page highlighting gig-worker exploitation issues  
210: - [x] Create `/restaurants` page explaining impact on local businesses  
211: - [x] Create `/alternatives` page showcasing better delivery options  
212: - [x] Create `/pledge` page with form and reasons to join the movement  
213: - [x] Create `/contact` page for inquiries, media, and partnerships  
214: - [x] Create `/privacy` page for GDPR compliance  
215: - [x] Create `/success-stories` page for restaurants that left Wolt  
216: - [ ] Extract and organize English content from wolt.md for all pages  
217: - [ ] Extract and organize Danish content from wolt.md for all pages  
218: - [x] Implement language switcher component that preserves current page  
219: - [x] Create reusable `<Citation>` component for sourcing all claims  
220: - [ ] Add comparison tables showing Wolt fees vs competitors  
221: - [ ] Add responsive hero illustrations for each main content page  
222: - [ ] Implement "Share this fact" social media buttons on key statistics  
223: - [ ] Add animated scroll-triggered fact reveals on scroll  
224: - [ ] Integrate Markdown parser for long-form blog posts (`next-mdx-remote`)  
225: 
226: ### 📝 Pledge Flow
227: - [ ] Build **PledgeModal** with name + email fields, hCaptcha token  
228: - [ ] Add `/api/pledge` route: validate, insert row, send verify email  
229: - [ ] Configure Supabase email template with DK/EN dynamic content  
230: - [ ] Implement `/verify?token=` page to confirm pledge and show thanks  
231: - [ ] Build real-time **PledgeCounter** using Supabase Realtime subscription  
232: - [ ] Add toast notifications for success / validation errors  
233: 
234: ### 🗺️ Alternatives Finder
235: - [ ] Create `/alternatives` route with city search input  
236: - [ ] Fetch Just Eat open data endpoint and cache result in KV store  
237: - [ ] Render results in responsive cards with rating, pickup option flag  
238: - [ ] Add "Call restaurant" link with `tel:` for mobile users  
239: - [ ] Log `alternative_click` event to analytics  
240: 
241: ### 🔍 SEO & Performance
242: - [ ] Generate XML sitemap at build time and upload to `/public`  
243: - [ ] Create `robots.txt` disallowing crawl on staging  
244: - [ ] Lazy-load all images with blur-up placeholder  
245: - [ ] Implement critical CSS extraction (`next/critical-css` plugin)  
246: - [ ] Verify LCP < 2 s on desktop via Lighthouse CI in GitHub Action  
247: 
248: ### ♿ Accessibility
249: - [ ] Add skip-to-content link at top of page  
250: - [ ] Ensure all interactive elements have `aria-*` labels  
251: - [ ] Check colour contrast ratios meet WCAG 2.1 AA  
252: - [ ] Test keyboard navigation path end-to-end with Cypress axe plugin  
253: 
254: ### 📊 Analytics & Monitoring
255: - [ ] Embed **Plausible** script with `self-hosted` proxy option  
256: - [ ] Track custom events: `pledge_started`, `pledge_verified`, `alt_click`  
257: - [ ] Schedule daily export job to BigQuery via Plausible API  
258: - [ ] Enable Vercel **Web Vitals** and error logging  
259: - [ ] Configure Slack webhook for production deployment alerts  
260: 
261: ### ✅ Testing
262: - [ ] Add **Jest** + `@testing-library/react` and first render test  
263: - [ ] Mock Supabase client in unit tests  
264: - [ ] Write Playwright e2e: landing → pledge → verify happy path  
265: - [ ] Add Axe accessibility check to Playwright run  
266: - [ ] Collect code-coverage and fail CI under 80 % lines  
267: 
268: ### 🚀 Release & Ops
269: - [ ] Create `production` branch protection rules (2 approvals)  
270: - [ ] Set up Vercel Preview Comments on PRs  
271: - [ ] Configure Vercel Cron job to re-validate static props nightly  
272: - [ ] Write `README.md` with local dev, staging, prod instructions  
273: - [ ] Tag `v1.0.0` and create GitHub Release notes after QA sign-off  
274: 
275: ### 🛡️ Legal & Compliance
276: - [ ] Draft privacy policy page (GDPR compliant)  
277: - [ ] Add cookie-consent banner using `cookie-consent` React component  
278: - [ ] Vet all claims with legal; add inline citations to sources list  
279: - [ ] Host Creative Commons licence for content reuse  
280: 
281: ### 🧳 Nice-to-Have (Stretch)
282: - [ ] Implement dark-mode auto-switch based on `prefers-color-scheme`  
283: - [ ] Add "Hall of Shame" carousel of Wolt's controversies with citations  
284: - [ ] Build blog CMS integration via Contentful webhook to ISR route  
285: - [ ] Add progressive-web-app manifest and service-worker for offline reading  
286: 
287: All boxes start unchecked so your delivery team can plan and track progress sprint-by-sprint.
